title: #incidents: 6865432109
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident 6865432109 - Dedicated pool us-west-2 experiencing p99 latency spike (2.8s) and streaming stalls. Autoscaler warmup delays detected. Paging oncall + eng-sre. Dash: https://dashboards.redwood/incident/6865432109

Alex Cheng: ack, I'm on call. seeing spike on the graphs now. p95 ~800ms, p99 ~2.8s, backlog queue depth jumped to ~12k. :grimacing:

Jordan Wells: we've got 3 paying tenants reporting degraded streaming (long stalls then reconnect). CS notified. Trying to gather which customers exactly now.

Priya Natarajan: logging in to control plane now. first pass: what changed in last deploy? any model variant rollouts or kernel changes?

Miguel Torres: nothing rolling in prod in last 2 hours AFAIK. runtime telemetry shows a surge of warmup failures, handshake times spiking. suspect warmup slot starvation in us-west pool.

Samir Patel: checking autoscaler logs and pool state. pulling current desired vs running:```json
{"pool":"us-west-2-dedicated","desired_replicas":24,"ready_replicas":18,"warming_replicas":3,"pending_warmups":9,"queue_depth":12134}```

Priya Natarajan: pending_warmups 9 while ready < desired -> autoscaler triggered but warmup latency high. Any kv-cache coldpath or heavy quantized loads?

Miguel Torres: runtime logs show repeated handshake timeouts (~12s) for warmup tasks. excerpt:```
[2026-03-15T10:12:03Z] warmup: slot_alloc failed handshake_timeout_ms=12000 model_variant=redwood-quant-v2
[2026-03-15T10:12:04Z] warmup: retrying slot_alloc
```

Alex Cheng: could be kernel selection regression on the new runtime build. Sam, can you check node GPU load and if any nodes stuck in init state?

Samir Patel: running node check:```
redwoodctl nodes list --pool us-west-2-dedicated
# output (trimmed)
node-42 gpu_util=98% status=ready
node-43 gpu_util=95% status=ready
node-47 gpu_util=3%  status=initializing (warmup hung)
node-49 gpu_util=2%  status=initializing (warmup hung)
```
Looks like 2 nodes stuck initializing, not accepting warmups. Investigating kube events.

Leah Kim: customers on the hotshot tier will notice first. Can we route their traffic to a fallback region or a smaller model variant temporarily?

Alex Cheng: immediate mitigations: 1) scale reserve capacity up (manual), 2) enable fallback to eu-west for non-data-residency tenants, 3) reduce concurrency for streaming to shed load. I'm going to manually bump pool desired to 30 to force more provisioning.

Priya Natarajan: manual bump ok, but warmup still needs to finish. Suggest we also toggle warmup_preload=false for a minute so we don't block on full model load for some requests. That would trade latency for correctness but reduce handshake pressure.

Miguel Torres: agree. toggling warmup_preload for hotshot routing paths. issuing command:```bash
redwoodctl config set --pool us-west-2-dedicated warmup_preload=false --reason "live mitigation 6865432109"
```

Samir Patel: bumped desired_replicas to 30. provisioning started. kube events show nodes spinning up, but a lot depends on image pull and kernel module init times (we're seeing a slow init on a subset).

Jordan Wells: notifying affected customers: "We're investigating increased streaming latency in US-West. We've routed traffic to fallback capacity and are working on mitigation. Will update in 15m."

incident-bot: Notification: oncall escalation to eng-sre@redwood for p99 breach. Runbook link: https://runbooks.redwood/pools/warmup-starvation

Alex Cheng: quick test of failover routing: I toggled a small percentage of traffic to eu-west and p99 for that shard dropped to 580ms. Good signal.

Priya Natarajan: current telemetry after mitigations: p95=420ms, p99=1.1s, queue_depth=4.2k, pending_warmups=3. Improvement but still above SLO at p99.

Miguel Torres: starting to roll a temporary kernel fallback on 4 nodes (previous stable kernel) to see if handshake timeouts stop. Will coordinate with infra.

Samir Patel: kernel rollback staged to node-47,node-49,node-51,node-52. Applying one-by-one to avoid mass restart. If rollback clears init hang, warmup should catch up.

Leah Kim: product wants a customer impact summary when we have a stable snapshot. Also flagging this for the weekly reliability review.

Alex Cheng: status update: kernel rollback completed on node-47 and node-49. warmup failures for those nodes dropped, pending_warmups->1, queue_depth->1800. p99 trending toward 750ms.

Jordan Wells: CS: two customers reported timeouts earlier, both on hotshot; we've confirmed retries for streaming succeeded after failover. No financial data impacted.

Priya Natarajan: next steps: 1) keep fallback routing until pool warmups finish, 2) collect warmup handshake traces and attach to incident, 3) run postmortem to determine why image init/kmod path regressed.

Miguel Torres: grabbing traces now, will upload to the incident timeline. Also scheduling a canary roll of the new runtime build into a small pool to reproduce locally.

Alex Cheng: handing over to eng-sre for post-incident follow-up. If anything regresses ping this thread. Thanks all. :heartpulse:

Leah Kim: noted — product will draft comms for impacted customers and a short status update for slack channels once postmortem is ready.

incident-bot: Incident 6865432109 - mitigation in progress. Current severity: Sev2. Open tasks: attach traces, postmortem owner, update runbook with warmup rollback play.

Alex Cheng: postmortem owner: Priya. I'll close paging once p99 < 1s for 15m sustained.

Priya Natarajan: taking postmortem ownership. ETA for initial writeup 24h. Closing updates for now — leaving thread open for any followups.

title: #incidents: 3905553333
preview:
oncall-bot: ALERT: High NXDOMAIN rate detected for api.redwood-inference.com from EU resolver pool (eu-west-*). Severity: P1. Trigger: dns-monitoring rule dns.nxdomain.rate > 5% for 3m. Link: https://ops.redwood/internal/incidents/3905553333
oncall-bot: Incident channel auto-created: #inc-3905553333 (paging SRE oncall)
Erin Nguyen: acked. I'm on the line and pulling graphs. Seeing spike starting ~3905553200 (approx 8m ago).
Maya Patel: looking at resolver probes now. Quick dig from public resolvers shows NXDOMAIN from some IPs, SERVFAIL from others. Copying samples:
```
;; Querying 1.1.1.1
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NXDOMAIN, id: 12345
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;api.redwood-inference.com. IN A
```
```
;; Querying 34.216.34.22 (eu resolver)
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: SERVFAIL, id: 67890
```
Maya Patel: early hypothesis: partial zone push to authoritative cluster caused inconsistent serials between authoritative name servers -> some NSs answering NXDOMAIN while others fine. Sounds like a race during zone reload.
Jordan Kline: confirmed from our side — pushing zones to provider-auth1 and provider-auth2. provider-auth1 rolled successfully, provider-auth2 got a transient failure during transfer and rolled back to empty zone for a few minutes (we see serial 0 on some servers). This leads to NXDOMAIN for delegated records.
Ben Carter: customer impact: internal telemetry shows ~7% of API hostname lookups from EU edge failing with NXDOMAIN -> causes client errors and SAML auth flips for EU customers. Support flagged 12 tickets so far.
Maya Patel: mitigation plan: 1) stop further zone updates, 2) roll back to last known-good serial on provider-auth2, 3) temporarily lower TTLs won't help here (they control caching) but we can ask edge cache to respect stale-while-revalidate. Also prepare CNAME fallback for the most critical subdomain.
Jordan Kline: executing emergency rollback to serial 2026040103 on provider-auth2 via API now. Command used:
```
curl -X POST https://api.authoritative-provider.example.com/zones/zoneid/rollback \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $PROVIDER_TOKEN' \
  -d '{"serial":2026040103}'
```
Jordan Kline: rollback accepted by provider API, propagation in progress. Notifying Cloudflare/Route53 federated cache to refresh via purge endpoints.
oncall-bot: deploy-bot: zone rollback job started (job id 98321).
Lucy Zhao: good. Opened a provider support ticket and requested a contact escalation. Ticket 98765. Mentioned urgent business impact and asked for confirmation of which NSs served empty zone during the window.
Maya Patel: metrics check 5m after rollback: NXDOMAIN rate down from 18% to 3% in EU. Some resolvers still returning cached NXDOMAIN; TTL and negative caching make this noisy. Proposing temporary traffic mitigation: adjust GeoDNS routing to prefer secondaries that served correct zone earlier, and add short-lived CNAME fallback for auth.example endpoints to fallback-host.internal.redwood.
Ben Carter: support comms: drafting banner and outbound note. Core content: impacted regions (EU), affected endpoints (api.redwood-inference.com and auth.redwood-inference.com), mitigation taken (rollback to known good zone, contacting providers), ETA for full resolution: monitor 30-60m for negative-cache expiry.
Maya Patel: additional technical note: negative caching (SOA negative TTL / NXTTL) was 3600s on the bad serial; provider is lowering negative TTL to 60s for emergency to speed recovery. They confirmed the change.
Erin Nguyen: thanks. Can we also enable 'serve-stale' on our DNS resolvers so edge returns last good answer for a short period while upstream heals? That will reduce customer impact for read-heavy paths.
Jordan Kline: serve-stale enabled on our resolvers (config push id 7742). Monitoring.
Leo Ramos: I'm the next oncall, will take handoff in 15m. Leaving runbook notes: rollback executed, provider ticket 98765, purge requests sent, serve-stale enabled, monitoring dashboards updated. Will watch for recurrence on zone pushes.
Maya Patel: creating follow-up action items for postmortem: 1) provider transfer retry logic - make rollbacks atomic, 2) test harness for zone push under load, 3) update runbook to include provider-side negative TTL adjustments, 4) ensure alerts fire on serial mismatch across NS set.
Support Bot: Support draft prepared and pinned in channel for review.
Ben Carter: marking comms ready for send after Lucy review. Lucy, can you review and ok?
Lucy Zhao: reviewing now, will sign off in 5m.
Erin Nguyen: immediate status: incident now in monitoring (P1 -> monitoring). NXDOMAIN rate trending down. Will keep channel open for 60m then update status to resolved if no regression.
Leo Ramos: ack, I'll escalate immediately if rate rises above 5% again. Handing off on-call to Leo now (coming up). :handshake:
Maya Patel: thanks all — adding postmortem owner tag to Leo.
oncall-bot: INCIDENT UPDATE: Severity changed to MONITORING at 3905553440. Close/resolve steps pending postmortem.

title: #incidents: 3765432109
preview:
incident-bot: [INCIDENT] cont-batch KV-cache growth detected on cluster us-west-2 - high eviction rate, latencies rising. Severity: P1?
Ava Chen: saw requests spiking, tail latency > 800ms on serving-rt-3, paged KVstore heavily. Repro starts ~12:13 UTC
Jon Martinez: oncall here, details? metrics link: https://grafana/redwood/d/abc123/contbatch?orgId=1
Mika Patel: customer reports: AcmeCorp API timeouts; BetaHealth seeing 5xx spikes. Paging now.
Priya Kapoor: starting triage - checking continuous-batching config across pools
Ava Chen: evidence suggests max_batch_tokens is set to 65536 in some pools; KV cache growth -> eviction storms -> paging.
Jon Martinez: ugh, we rolled that experimental config two days ago (autotune PR #412). Rolling it back.
deploy-bot: deploy started by @Jon Martinez: rollback config autotune -> stable-config (deploy id d-8842)
Priya Kapoor: plan: 1) throttle cont-batching for affected models 2) lower max_batch_tokens 3) enforce max_num_seqs per batch 4) increase KV eviction threshold temporarily
Jon Martinez: executing hotfix now — setting `max_batch_tokens=8192` and `max_num_seqs=12` for pool cont-batcher-usw2
Ava Chen: command I ran:
```
redwoodctl config set cont-batcher-usw2 max_batch_tokens=8192 max_num_seqs=12 --env prod
redwoodctl service restart cont-batcher-usw2 --graceful
```
Mika Patel: pushing customer comms draft to #customer-success, tagging affected customers: AcmeCorp, BetaHealth
Priya Kapoor: monitoring KPIs. watch for eviction_rate, tail_latency, 5xx_rate
Jon Martinez: initial results: eviction_rate down 70%, p95 latency from 820ms -> 240ms in 6m
Ava Chen: BetaHealth still seeing elevated 502s, sending logs to Eng Support
deploy-bot: canary rollout of config complete on 3 nodes (cont-batcher-usw2-canary)
Priya Kapoor: disabling continuous batching for highest-traffic model (model-id: text-redwood-large-v2) as extra safety
Jon Martinez: config diff we applied:
```
- max_batch_tokens: 65536
- max_num_seqs: 64
+ max_batch_tokens: 8192
+ max_num_seqs: 12
+ continuous_batching.enabled: true (but disabled for text-redwood-large-v2)
```
Ava Chen: customer impact assessment: ~2 customers degraded (AcmeCorp, BetaHealth), approx 18 minutes of increased errors during window 12:13-12:31 UTC. No data loss. Classification: P2 -> escalated because customer-facing errors.
Mika Patel: posted incident notification to customers at 12:28 UTC. AcmeCorp support ticket #AC-992
Priya Kapoor: next actions: run postmortem, revert autotune default, add guardrail to prevent max_batch_tokens > 16384, add alert for kv_eviction_rate > 0.2
Jon Martinez: handing off to Priya for RCA and timeline. I'm on call until 13:00 UTC and can help with follow-ups.
Priya Kapoor: assigning owner: @Priya Kapoor (RCA), SRE follow-ups -> @Ava Chen for runbooks, CS follow-up -> @Mika Patel
Ava Chen: quick note — please add a regression test to canary pipeline that includes KV pressure scenario
Priya Kapoor: creating incident doc: https://confluence.redwood/incidents/3765432109-contbatch-kv-growth
incident-bot: Incident acknowledged. Status: mitigation applied, monitoring. Handoff: Priya Kapoor
Jon Martinez: thx all. closing live channel, will reopen if metrics worsen :thumbsup:
Mika Patel: creating follow-up customer email template and internal blameless RCA invite
Priya Kapoor: timeline saved, will circulate draft RCA by EOD
Ava Chen: logs snippet (post-hotfix):
```
[12:22:03] eviction_rate: 0.03
[12:22:18] p95_latency_ms: 240
[12:22:30] 5xx_rate: 0.8%
```
Jon Martinez: policy note: limit max_batch_tokens default <= 16384, and add validation in config pipeline
Mika Patel: tagging @support to ensure tickets are tracked
Priya Kapoor: will schedule a follow-up runbook update and a postmortem review w/ eng-runtime next week
incident-bot: Incident thread closed (mitigated). Remain on-call for 30min for watch. :white_check_mark:

title: #incidents: 3380012345
preview:
OpsBot: ALERT: ACME Corp (tenant: acme-prod-vpc) - automated eval detected a quality regression for model `redwood-v2-quant-8bit` on dedicated pool. Relative score drop: ~18% on invoice_e2e eval. Triggered 2026-03-15T10:12Z. Paging on-call.
Diego Ramos: oncall here, seeing the page. I'll pull logs and current quant profile.
Diego Ramos: @Mira Chen can you confirm if any recent rollout touched tenant-specific quant profiles?
Mira Chen: I didn't push any tenant-specific changes today. We did a global push of a new quant kernel at 09:50 but it should be opt-in per-tenant; double-checking.
Tariq Patel: I'm looking at the dedicated pool for acme-prod-vpc. Host nodes show recent restart around 09:53, but pods came back green. No infra resource pressure spikes.
Ben Carter: Customer CS: ACME reported a spike in "nonsense" tokens in generated invoices at 10:18 UTC. They pegged it to our responses being truncated/incorrect line items. Impacting production.
Mira Chen: Ok — likely a mismatched quant profile or kernel path being used for that tenant. Plan options: (1) revert tenant quant profile to previous working profile; (2) route ACME to hosted float16 model as temporary fallback; (3) short-term rate-limit to reduce noise while we validate. Thoughts?
Alex Rivera: priority 1 is restore quality for customer. Revert tenant profile first. If that fails, route to hosted variant. Need comms to ACME either way.
Diego Ramos: I'll try revert first. Sharing commands I'll run now:```
# revert quant profile for tenant
curl -X POST "https://controlplane.redwood.internal/tenants/acme-prod-vpc/quant_profiles/revert" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $REVERT_TOKEN" \
  -d '{"target_version":"profile-1.2"}'
# restart routing for tenant service
kubectl -n dedicated-acme rollout restart deployment/acme-serving
```
Diego Ramos: running revert now. ETA ~3-4m.
OpsBot: ACTION: Handoff note added to incident log. Severity: Customer-Impact (P1).
Diego Ramos: revert request succeeded (HTTP 202). Restarting deployment...
Diego Ramos: restart complete, doing quick smoke eval against invoice_e2e on canary shard.
Mira Chen: I'll run a small eval with the previous golden prompt set and compare token-level metrics.
Mira Chen: eval result: quality metrics back to baseline within noise (~+1% vs pre-regression).
Ben Carter: ACME on phone — they confirm responses look normal now. Thanks. They're asking for a timeline and root cause.
Tariq Patel: infra logs show the dedicated pool picked up the new global quant kernel at 09:50 but our per-tenant selector rule failed to prefer the tenant's pinned profile because of a config race during node reboot. In short: kernel advertised globally before tenant config reconciled.
Alex Rivera: good find. Immediate mitigation = confirmed revert executed and canary eval green. Next: patch reconciliation order and add an extra check before global kernel advertisement.
Katie Liu: I'll draft the customer note: scope, timeline, mitigation, and next steps. Ben, want me to send to ACME directly or you handle?
Ben Carter: please send — include that we reverted tenant quant profile to profile-1.2, mitigation window 10:12-10:46 UTC, and that we will follow up with a postmortem and permanent fix.
Diego Ramos: adding runbook entry for tenant-specific rollback steps. Snippet: ```
# rollback runbook excerpt
1) POST /tenants/<tenant>/quant_profiles/revert {target_version}
2) kubectl -n dedicated-<tenant> rollout restart deployment/<serving>
3) run eval: curl http://eval-service/score?set=invoice_e2e&model=<model>
4) confirm metrics within SLA
```
Mira Chen: also adding automated alert to detect when a global kernel advertisement happens within 5m of node restarts for a tenant pool.
OpsBot: NOTE: Oncall Handoff planned at 11:00 UTC. Diego -> Mira for follow-up and postmortem.
Diego Ramos: handing off to @Mira Chen for postmortem. I've attached logs and the revert request id: revert-20260315-ACME-01.
Mira Chen: acknowledged, taking lead on postmortem. I'll coordinate with Tariq for infra timeline and Alex for product action items.
Ben Carter: customer notified at 10:55 UTC. They appreciated the quick fix.
Alex Rivera: action items assigned: Mira (postmortem + reconciliation change), Tariq (infra gating + alerts), Diego (document rollback runbook), Katie (customer comms).
Mira Chen: initial postmortem draft incoming within 24h. Will include root cause, impact window, and verification artifacts.
OpsBot: INCIDENT STATUS: mitigated. Summary: tenant quant profile reverted -> quality restored. Customer impact window 10:12 - 10:46 UTC. Postmortem required.
Diego Ramos: closing my oncall loop. Ping me if anything regresses.
Mira Chen: thanks team.
Katie Liu: sent ACME note, included rollback details and next steps.
Ben Carter: ACME replied with :thumbsup:.
Mira Chen: last check — automated eval after 30m shows stable metrics. Closing thread once PM review complete.

title: #incidents: 3337778888
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: High GPU OOM rate on decode/stream pool for route /v1/generate. Request drops increasing. Metric: decode_gpu_ooms=18/min, dropped_requests=7%. Paging on-call.
Alice Nguyen: I'm on it. quick summary? which route + customers impacted?
incident-bot: Route: /v1/generate. Cluster: decode-prod-us-west. First spike @3337778860. Affected model: rdx-large-decoder-v2 and its quantized fallback.
Samir Patel: seeing repeated `max_tokens=null` in incoming requests for a subset of traffic — looks like SDK edge-case not setting token cap. decode loop keeps allocating KV cache until CUDA OOM.
Javier Morales: logs attached (excerpt):
```
E 2025-11-02T18:21:43Z CudaError: out of memory at decode.cu:421
GPU 0: 11.9 GiB used of 12.0 GiB
Trace: decode_step -> attention_kv_alloc -> cudaMalloc failed
```
Javier Morales: this is triggering container restarts on 4 pods; 2 nodes have flapped in the last 12m. :fire:
Priya Kaur: immediate mitigation options? Can we enforce a hard token cap at the routing layer and route offenders to a safe model?
Samir Patel: yes. plan: 1) enforce route-level max_tokens=512 2) enable fallback to rdx-large-quantized 3) throttle burst concurrency for route 4) cordon/drain flapping nodes.
Samir Patel: applying route cap now. command: 
```
redwoodctl policy update --route /v1/generate --set max_tokens=512 --enforce=true
```
Maya Chen: support: 12 open user tickets referencing 503/empty responses. 3 customers report production impact (one >50% errors). Escalating CS priority.
Ben Carter: notified the larger customer (AcmeCorp) and opened a bridge. They're seeing ~60% failures on streaming calls.
Samir Patel: also enabling quick fallback policy: route will try rdx-large-decoder-v2 then quantized variant if decode allocation exceeds threshold. patch: 
```
redwoodctl rollout create --route /v1/generate --fallback rdx-large-quantized --threshold 0.75
```
deploy-bot: rollout 56f4a initiated for /v1/generate by @Samir. Progressive canary starting (10% -> 50% -> 100%).
Javier Morales: started cordon/drain for nodes g-decode-07 and g-decode-09. Evicting affected pods and moving load to healthy nodes.
Priya Kaur: applying emergency throttle for burst concurrency: 
```
redwoodctl config set /routes/generate/concurrency_burst 4
```
Alice Nguyen: prepping handoff. I'm going to collect checklist: monitoring links, recent logs, mitigation commands, customer list.
Samir Patel: quick patch pushed to runtime: enforce in-request token cap before allocation (hotfix id hf-2025-11-02-01). It will reject requests with tokens >512 with a 400 and reason `tokencap_enforced`.
Maya Chen: support update: after cap applied error rate dropped to 18% then 6% over 8 minutes. Still seeing node restarts but fewer OOMs.
Javier Morales: observed node-level pod evictions stabilizing. OOMs per minute down from 18 to 2. GPU memory pressure reduced.
Ben Carter: AcmeCorp seeing improvement to ~10% failure; they've paused a heavy job while we stabilize.
Lina Ortiz: product asks for quick root cause notes: is this SDK default behavior or client-side bug?
Samir Patel: initial analysis: mix — some clients passed explicit null/0 or omitted max_tokens and our legacy default allowed unbounded. We'll update SDK defaults and server-side enforcement.
Alice Nguyen: handoff time: I'll hand to night oncall (Priya) at ts=3337779000. Priya, you'll take over monitoring cadence and customer comms.
Priya Kaur: confirmed. I'll be on for the next 12h. Please add runbook link and escalation contacts.
Alice Nguyen: runbook: https://internal.redwood/runbooks/decode-gpu-oom (added to thread). Also opened incident-1358 in tracker.
Samir Patel: post-incident actions assigned: SDK default change -> @Lina, server enforce tokencap -> @Samir, customer communications follow-up -> @Ben, postmortem owner -> @Javier.
deploy-bot: rollout 56f4a reached 100% at 3337779150.
Maya Chen: closing interim support update: most customers confirmed degraded but recovering. Continued monitoring for 2h.
Javier Morales: cordoned nodes uncordon scheduled after stabilization checks. If OOMs spike again, we'll rollback to reduced traffic mode.
Priya Kaur: taking handoff now. All actions and commands are in the runbook. I'll update metrics every 15m.
Alice Nguyen: thanks team. I'll file a brief incident summary for execs and add timeline.
incident-bot: INCIDENT: mitigations applied, paging suspended. Please continue to post updates in this thread for the next 2 hours. :white_check_mark:

title: #incidents: 3245678123
preview:
incident-bot: PagerDuty page fired: INC-2026 - High inference latency (eu-west-1) -> multiple customers reporting slow responses, alert rate spike x120 in 5m. https://pagerduty.example/INC-2026 :rotating_light:
Ava Chen: acked, I'm on it — pulling up traces and per-route SLOs now. tagging the runbook. :mag:
Diego Martinez: ack. I'm pulling the customer list and recent deploys. can someone check dedicated pools and autoscaler status?
Priya Nair: checking dedicated GPU pools in eu-west-1 — one pool (reserved-pool-eu-2) at 92% GPU util, others 60-70%. Not seeing recent deploys in last 30m. Query: `redwoodctl clusters metrics --region eu-west-1 --metric gpu_util --last 10m`
Priya Nair: quick pilot check logs show kv-cache eviction warnings on nodes: ```
Evicting KV cache entry for model: open-4m-v1, reason=memory_pressure
2026-03-15T22:01:10Z WARN kvcache: eviction_count=78
```
Ava Chen: hmm, KV eviction could explain repeated recompute and latency. Diego — which customers are hit?
Diego Martinez: impacted customers from top of log: [AcmeCorp, NovoHealth, GlidePay]. Error budgets spiking for embed endpoint on AcmeCorp's org key. Slack thread from customer: "embedding calls >2s".
Luca Rossi: @Ava I can take this in 10m if you want to handoff; have stable VPN to eu-west infra.
Ava Chen: thanks @Luca — I'm going to do a quick mitigation to reduce tail latency. plan: 1) scale reserved-pool-eu-2 by +2 nodes (temporary), 2) pin fallback routing to quantized variant for embed route, 3) rehydrate hot KV prefixes. Doing step 1 now.
Ava Chen: running scale command: ```
# scale dedicated pool
redwoodctl pool scale reserved-pool-eu-2 --add 2 --reason \"INC-2026 emergency scale\"
```
SRE-Shift-Bot: autoscaler event: pool reserved-pool-eu-2 scale request accepted -> provisioning 2 nodes (expected ready ~3m). :gear:
Priya Nair: while nodes come up, I'm initiating kvcache warmup for top prefixes for impacted models. script: ```
curl -X POST https://infra.internal/kvcache/rehydrate -d '{"model":"open-4m-v1","prefixes":["customer:acme:","customer:novo:"]}'
```
Diego Martinez: docs links and perf dash: https://grafana.example/d/latency/embeds?org=acme (watching spikes). Customer comms: I'll draft a short note and flag that we're on it.
Ava Chen: nodes reported ready. provisioning logs: `node-34 ready, node-35 ready` — scaling complete. rerouting to fallback quantized variant now.
Luca Rossi: ack taking over the bridge. I'll run a quick end-to-end test against AcmeCorp's test key and watch tail latencies.
Luca Rossi: test run: 50 requests -> p95 300ms, p99 1.1s, previous p99 ~2.8s. Looks improved. :white_check_mark:
Diego Martinez: customer comms sent: "We're investigating high latency and have applied mitigations; monitoring. We'll follow up with a post-incident summary." Customer support ticket attached.
Ava Chen: handoff notes for @Luca: what I did -> scaled reserved-pool-eu-2 +2 nodes, applied fallback routing to quantized variant for embed route, initiated KV rehydrate for top customer prefixes. Open follow-ups: monitor for reoccurrence, check if autoscaler thresholds need tuning, and assess whether the eviction indicates memory leak in recent runtime.
Luca Rossi: received. I'll keep the bridge for 40m and then pass back if stable. tagging SRE follow-up owners: @Priya for kvcache investigation, @Diego for customer impact summary.
SRE-Shift-Bot: reminder: please run the incident impact checklist and file an incident report: /incidents/new INC-2026 -> severity: Sev2 if >10% customers degraded for >15m.
Priya Nair: capturing logs + creating an artifact with KV eviction traces; linking: https://artifacts.internal/INC-2026/kv-evictions.log
Diego Martinez: last check — embed error rate down 90%, latency returning to normal for majority of routes. will update customer channel in 10m.
Luca Rossi: final note before I hand back to Ava: sustained p99 reduced, no new evictions in last 8m, autoscaler added nodes, rollback not required. leaving sticky note for postmortem.
Ava Chen: thanks team. I'll pick up postmortem tomorrow and include remediation options (autoscaler hysteresis, kvcache backpressure metrics, routing fallback policy sanity checks). For now closing the active page.
incident-bot: PagerDuty update: INC-2026 status -> resolved (mitigated). Closing alert window. :white_check_mark:

title: #incidents: 3180501234
preview:
incident-bot: 🔔 Pager P1: Serving errors spike in hosted inference (error rate 15%, latency 95p > 1.5s). Auto-assigned to oncall: Aisha Khan.

Aisha Khan: acked, looking. seeing sustained 5xx on /v1/generate across us-west. Anyone on-call for autoscaler? @Miguel?

Miguel Ortiz: on it. I'm seeing lots of node flaps in the inference node pool over last 4 mins. kube-events show repeated OOMKills on inference pods then HPA scaling up then immediate restarts. Suspect rapid scale up/down feedback.

Priya Shah: logs from one pod: 
```
2026-03-15T10:03:15Z ERROR ServingRuntime - CUDA_ERROR_OUT_OF_MEMORY: failed to allocate 2.3G
2026-03-15T10:03:15Z INFO Reconnecting to model shard 0
2026-03-15T10:03:16Z WARN PodRestart - CrashLoopBackOff
```

Ben Carter: confirmed. HPA target CPU spikes to 90% then drops, HPA ups replicas to 12, but new pods hit OOM during initialization and cause node GPU memory fragmentation -> kubelet evicts older pods -> cascade.

Raj Patel: that's the feedback loop: autoscaler sees CPU/latency spike -> scales -> warmup alloc causes CUDA OOM -> pods crash -> metrics bounce -> more scaling. Need to break it.

oncall-eu: customers reporting 502s, some dedicated customers routed to dedicated capacity, hosted customers with bursty traffic affected. Current 5xx rate ~14.8% (15m average).

Aisha Khan: immediate mitigations: 1) pause HPA, 2) reduce target replicas to a safe steady-state, 3) drain/cordon problematic nodes, 4) route traffic to fallback pool. Miguel / Priya take the actions, I'll coordinate customer comms.

Miguel Ortiz: executing: `kubectl patch hpa inference-hpa -n prod -p '{"spec":{"minReplicas":3,"maxReplicas":3}}'` to freeze scaling to 3 (will force steady state). Also adding annotation to disable autoscaling controller sync temporarily.

Priya Shah: cordoning nodes with repeated GPU OOM: `kubectl cordon gke-infer-07 gke-infer-09` and `kubectl drain gke-infer-09 --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data`. Marking node as under-investigation.

SRE-bot: Deploy bot detected a config change 3180501248 by Miguel Ortiz: patched HPA min/max -> 3.

Ben Carter: switching routing: flipping canary traffic to dedicated pool for latency-sensitive routes (`/v1/stream` and `/v1/chat`). Patch applied in route table, currently shifting ~40% to dedicated. Monitoring error rate.

Aisha Khan: Product/Support: please prepare status update. Priya, what's the error-rate trend after HPA freeze & cordon?

Priya Shah: after freeze + cordon + route shift (5m in): overall 5xx dropped from 15% -> 6.2%. Latency 95p back to 1.0s. But a subset of long-running batches still failing on legacy model variants.

Miguel Ortiz: additional action: disable prefix batching for the affected model family to reduce peak GPU memory during startup. Patch to runtime: `REDWOOD_BATCHER_DISABLE_PREFIX=true` for those deployment sets. Rolling restart of 3 pods in place.

Raj Patel: for longer-term safety during the incident, I'm lowering the per-pod memory request for the quantized model to avoid warmup fragmentation and enabling on-boot gc sweep: `--gc-aggressive` flag for runtime. We'll track OOMs.

Ben Carter: Monitoring update: after these mitigations, error rate steady ~3-4% and dropping. No new node flaps in last 3 minutes. We should be able to re-enable autoscaling carefully with a dampening window.

Aisha Khan: agree. Plan to re-enable with conservative params: target min 4 max 8, scaleUp stabilization 300s, scaleDown stabilization 600s. We'll also enable model fallback to quantized variant when OOMs observed. Miguel to apply.

Miguel Ortiz: applying conservative HPA: `kubectl patch hpa inference-hpa -p '{"spec":{"minReplicas":4,"maxReplicas":8}}'` and setting controller annotation `scale-stabilization: 300s`. Patch in prod at 10:18Z.

SRE-bot: Alert: new pod OOMs dropped below threshold.

Priya Shah: customer impact notes: we have 12 customers with elevated errors (>5% of requests). Two enterprise tenants experienced degraded SLA for ~12 minutes. Support is drafting messages for those two.

Aisha Khan: draft OK to send? Keep it factual: describe transient capacity instability, mitigation steps, and an ETA for follow-up postmortem. Priya, Ben coordinate the messages and include temporary routing workaround for customers who need immediate relief.

Raj Patel: post-incident remediation items (initial): 1) add HPA dampening defaults to all inference HPAs, 2) add startup memory reservation to avoid GPU fragmentation, 3) implement runtime safe-start (pre-allocate smaller tensors during init), 4) create automated rollback policy to quantized variant on OOM spike.

Miguel Ortiz: logging snippet for SOC: 
```
k8s_events | grep inference | tail -n 20
2026-03-15T10:05:12Z Pod inference-aaa OOMKilled container runtime
2026-03-15T10:05:13Z HPA inference-hpa scale to 12
2026-03-15T10:05:15Z Pod inference-bbb CrashLoopBackOff
2026-03-15T10:06:00Z Cordon node gke-infer-09
2026-03-15T10:07:10Z Route update applied -> shift 40% to dedicated
```

Ben Carter: ops note: post-restore we'll run a controlled scale test (simulate slow ramp) in staging with the exact model weights to validate warmup behavior before enabling aggressive autoscaling again.

Aisha Khan: thanks team. I'm escalating to incident-review. We'll keep HPA conservative for 2h and schedule a postmortem. I'll send a status update to #customers-incident-updates in 10m with impact summary. Oncall handed over to Miguel at 10:25Z once stabilised.

Miguel Ortiz: handing off monitoring to Raj for the next 2 hours. If any OOMs spike, auto-route to dedicated pool immediately.

Raj Patel: ack. Adding a transient alert policy: if CUDA_OOM events > 5/min -> open pager.

incident-bot: Incident P1 status updated: mitigated, monitoring. Timeline: root 3180501234, mitigations applied 3180501290 - 3180501580. Postmortem required.

Aisha Khan: closing thread here. Add any follow-ups to the postmortem checklist ticket. Thanks all. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 3098765432
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired -> service=hosted-inference severity=critical incident=PD-78321
incident-bot: Incident link: https://pagerduty.com/incidents/PD-78321
incident-bot: Routing to primary on-call (Ava Chen). Auto-escalation in 5m if unacknowledged.

Ava Chen: ack
Ava Chen: grabbing graphs, seeing a latency + 5xx spike in us-west-2. dashboards: https://grafana.redwood.example/d/hosted-inference

Marco Silva: joining. preliminary: increased tail latency, connection resets from runtime-batch-7, and some OOMs on node runtime-7b. tailing logs now
Marco Silva:```
2026-03-15T09:41:12Z WARN network.go:88 conn_reset host=runtime-7b req_id=xyz789
2026-03-15T09:41:13Z ERROR memory.go:54 alloc failed host=runtime-7b details="failed to allocate 512MB"
```

Priya Kumar: PD shows secondary notified but didn't acknowledge (mobile DND?). auto-escalation scheduled to manager in ~3m. tagging @Liam Green for awareness.

Ava Chen: opening a transient bridge (meet.redwood/bridge-78321). Marco, can you try a graceful drain of runtime-7b and recycle? If it's a memory leak, we should isolate that instance.

Marco Silva: running: `kubectl drain runtime-7b --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data; kubectl delete pod runtime-7b -n inference`. Will re-add once health checks pass.

Support Bot: Notifying top affected customers: cust_42 (hosted), cust_99 (hosted), cust_enterprise_acme (dedicated). Prepared template for status page draft.

Ava Chen: cust_enterprise_acme has a live deployment; pinging SEs and account lead. I'll draft a sitrep: brief summary, mitigation in progress, ETA 10m to validate.

incident-bot: PagerDuty escalation: secondary timed out without ack -> manager escalation to Liam Green (PD-78321). Escalation URL: https://pagerduty.com/incidents/PD-78321/escalation

Liam Green: on the bridge. quick question: do we see a common model serving this traffic or is it a host-level issue?

Marco Silva: looks host-level; multiple models show failures but all passing through runtime-batch-7. suspect corrupted KV cache + memory fragmentation after heavy prefix caching.

Priya Kumar: documenting impact: cust_enterprise_acme ~8% error rate for last 12 minutes, cust_42 ~22% in us-west-2. Filing urgent CS ticket for cust_enterprise_acme.

Ava Chen: after recycle, runtime-7b came back healthy. I'm watching p50/p95 latency and 5xx.

Marco Silva: initial metrics: 5xx count dropped by ~80% in the recycled replica set within 90s. no new OOM lines in logs.

Ava Chen: rolling restart scheduled across runtime-batch-7 pool to ensure no other nodes are corrupted. command: `kubectl rollout restart deploy/runtime-batch-7 -n inference`

Liam Green: agree with targeted rollout. also open follow-up tasks: increase alerting sensitivity for early OOM signs, add out-of-band pager for manager on repeated secondary misses, and investigate caching eviction behavior.

incident-bot: PD-78321 acknowledged by Ava Chen at 2026-03-15T09:56:21Z. Incident state -> monitoring.

Ava Chen: status update for customers posted to status.redwood.example: "We experienced elevated error rates in hosted inference in us-west-2 between 09:36-09:50 UTC. Mitigation (pod recycle + targeted rollouts) reduced errors to baseline. Engineering investigating root cause."

Support Bot: Sent targeted customer email to cust_enterprise_acme and cust_42 with mitigation steps and contact info.

Priya Kumar: leaving notes: PD escalation worked as expected, but secondary missed ack due to DND. Recommend review of on-call contact tiers and fallback policy for mobiles.

Marco Silva: collecting heap profiles and heapdump from recovered node for postmortem. linking artifacts: s3://redwood-artifacts/PD-78321/

Liam Green: good containment. please create an incident postmortem doc and include timeline, mitigation commands, and follow-up action owners (alerting, runtime fixes, customer comms). Thanks team.

Ava Chen: will own the postmortem doc. leaving bridge open until 10:30 UTC for any regressions. :white_check_mark:

incident-bot: PD-78321 moved to monitoring at 2026-03-15T10:01:34Z.

title: #incidents: 2681234567
preview:
Incident Bot: PAGER: high error rate detected for customer AuroraCorp in aurora-vpc (dedicated pool). Alert id: AB-2026-418. Escalation policy: oncall-eng. Error = 502 / 5xx spike, started 03:24 UTC.
Ana Romero: oncall here, looking now. assigning to self.
Devon Li: +1, I'm nearby. initial triage: errors look like rpc timeouts from pool nodes, possibly network flapping between host-12 and host-13.
Ana Romero: running quick check on pool health and routing. tagging @Samantha for customer comms.
Ethan Park: saw the same in tracing — KV cache misses spiked at the same time. Could be overloaded proxy on host-13.
Devon Li: mitigation suggestion: failover traffic from host-13 and scale an extra replica. run: `infractl pool failover aurora-vpc --from host-13 --to host-15` then `infractl pool scale aurora-vpc --replicas 6`. i'll start the failover.
Ana Romero: executing now. pasting logs below.
```
01:03:12 infractl: initiating drain host-13
01:03:28 infractl: routing table update to host-15
01:03:45 monitor: 5xx rate dropping from 18% -> 4% within 20s
```
Devon Li: routing change completed. monitoring. 5xx down to ~3.8% and latency returning to baseline (p50 ~ 42ms).
Harper (ACME) - CS: Harper from AuroraCorp here — seeing errors on dashboard and some requests failing in prod. Any ETA? This is impacting search in the product, customers noticing.
Samantha Chen: Harper, we're actively mitigating and have failed over traffic away from the suspected host. initial ETA for stable baseline ~15–20m, we'll update you in 10. we know this customer is in a paid Dedicated plan — copying exec briefing in this thread after triage.
Harper (ACME) - CS: thanks. can you confirm if data was affected or only inference failures?
Ethan Park: looks like inference requests returned 5xx, no data plane writes affected. no KV corruption seen. traces show request timeouts, not model faults.
Raj Patel: I'll stand by to apply network ACL rollback if needed. current plan: keep host-13 drained and keep extra replica until we clear root cause.
Ana Romero: doc-ing immediate mitigation steps for handoff: 1) keep host-13 out of rotation, 2) scale to 6 replicas, 3) collect KV cache and network interface stats for host-13, 4) update customer comm draft. handoff to second oncall at 03:50 UTC.
Incident Bot: HANDOFF: oncall shift change at 03:50 UTC — new responder: Devon Li.
Devon Li: taking over. continuing root cause collection. I'm pulling pcap and netstack logs from host-13: `/var/logs/net-interfaces/host-13.pcap` and `dmesg`. will attach samples to ticket.
Samantha Chen: drafting customer comm for Harper/execs. proposed text: 

"AuroraCorp — update: At 03:24 UTC we detected an elevated error rate affecting inference calls routed to your aurora-vpc dedicated pool. We failed over traffic from one affected host and scaled the pool. The service is returning to normal; we are continuing investigations to determine the root cause. No customer data was modified. We will follow up with a detailed RCA and proposed credits under your SLA."

Samantha Chen: propose also a short exec brief for AuroraCorp CTO: 
"We experienced a transient network/host-level failure within AuroraCorp's dedicated inference pool. Immediate mitigation (host quarantine and scaling) restored normal service. Next steps: full RCA within 72 hours, timeline for remediation, and proposed service credit for 2 hours of downtime per SLA. Happy to join a 1:1 if useful."
Harper (ACME) - CS: that reads fine. can you add an ETA for the RCA? they will want a firm date. also include whether this will affect usage billing.
Samantha Chen: added ETA: RCA by EOD +72h (Friday 2026-04-10 17:00 UTC). billing: we will not bill for failed requests and will evaluate SLA credit. propose initial credit estimate: 2 hours of service credits (per plan).
Devon Li: captured artifacts and started RCA doc in docs/incidents/AB-2026-418-aurora. brief root cause hypothesis: intermittent NIC firmware bug + kernel interrupt storm leading to TCP stalls on host-13. evidence: repeated NIC resets in `dmesg` and interface counters jumping. attaching snippet:
```
[  812.445] e1000e 0000:02:00.0: Reset adapter
[  812.446] IPv4: eth0: NIC link down
[  812.473] netif: eth0: link up
[  812.892] watchdog: tx timeout on eth0
```
Ethan Park: +1 on NIC firmware suspicion. host-13 shows elevated interrupts and queue drops. need to coordinate with infra to schedule firmware patch/testing on similar hardware.
Raj Patel: opening infra ticket INF-2026-982 to track firmware remediation plan. linking to customer RCA.
Ana Romero: action items before handing off to post-incident comms: 
- Devon: finish full pcap + dmesg upload (due 04:20 UTC)
- Raj: infra ticket + firmware roll plan (due 04:45 UTC)
- Samantha: finalize customer comm and exec brief (send update to Harper and AuroraCorp execs by 04:00 UTC)
- Ethan: run health sweep across other hosts in aurora pool to confirm no siblings affected (due 04:15 UTC)

Ana Romero: leaving notes in ticket and assigning follow-up meeting for RCA review at 2026-04-12 14:00 UTC. please add any items.
Devon Li: uploaded pcap and dmesg to the incident doc. marking mitigation stable. 5xx now <0.5% for 15m.
Harper (ACME) - CS: appreciate the quick updates, thanks everyone. please confirm final customer note when sent.
Samantha Chen: sent customer update to Harper and execs at 03:58 UTC. copy of sent comm in the incident doc. proposed SLA credit workflow: CS to approve credit, finance to apply credit to next invoice, and post an update here once applied.
Raj Patel: infra ticket INF-2026-982 created. ETA: firmware window provisioning next week; will propose candidate hosts for canary.
Ana Romero: RCA timeline confirmed: preliminary RCA draft in 48h, final RCA + remediation plan in 72h. cross-team postmortem meeting scheduled.
Devon Li: tagging follow-ups: `#action: collect-kernel-logs`, `#action: run-firmware-canary`, `#action: customer-comm-sent`. closing live mitigation — monitoring for 1hr then will move to post-incident channel.
Incident Bot: INCIDENT: AB-2026-418 marked as mitigated at 04:18 UTC by Devon Li. next step: post-incident review.
Samantha Chen: noted. Harper — we'll follow up with the RCA and the proposed credit application. closing this channel for live paging; follow-ups will be in the incident doc and the scheduled RCA meeting.
Harper (ACME) - CS: thanks all — appreciate the visibility.
Raj Patel: adding final note: if we see weird NIC events on similar SKUs, open immediate page; otherwise proceed with planned canary patch next week.
Ana Romero: thanks team. will hand off remaining tasks to oncall rotation and CS for billing/credits."

Devon Li: taking over. continuing root cause collection. I'm pulling pcap and netstack logs from host-13: `/var/logs/net-interfaces/host-13.pcap` and `dmesg`. will attach samples to ticket.
Samantha Chen: drafting customer comm for Harper/execs. proposed text: 

"AuroraCorp — update: At 03:24 UTC we detected an elevated error rate affecting inference calls routed to your aurora-vpc dedicated pool. We failed over traffic from one affected host and scaled the pool. The service is returning to normal; we are continuing investigations to determine the root cause. No customer data was modified. We will follow up with a detailed RCA and proposed credits under your SLA."

Samantha Chen: propose also a short exec brief for AuroraCorp CTO: 
"We experienced a transient network/host-level failure within AuroraCorp's dedicated inference pool. Immediate mitigation (host quarantine and scaling) restored normal service. Next steps: full RCA within 72 hours, timeline for remediation, and proposed service credit for 2 hours of downtime per SLA. Happy to join a 1:1 if useful."
Harper (ACME) - CS: that reads fine. can you add an ETA for the RCA? they will want a firm date. also include whether this will affect usage billing.
Samantha Chen: added ETA: RCA by EOD +72h (Friday 2026-04-10 17:00 UTC). billing: we will not bill for failed requests and will evaluate SLA credit. propose initial credit estimate: 2 hours of service credits (per plan).
Devon Li: captured artifacts and started RCA doc in docs/incidents/AB-2026-418-aurora. brief root cause hypothesis: intermittent NIC firmware bug + kernel interrupt storm leading to TCP stalls on host-13. evidence: repeated NIC resets in `dmesg` and interface counters jumping. attaching snippet:
```
[  812.445] e1000e 0000:02:00.0: Reset adapter
[  812.446] IPv4: eth0: NIC link down
[  812.473] netif: eth0: link up
[  812.892] watchdog: tx timeout on eth0
```
Ethan Park: +1 on NIC firmware suspicion. host-13 shows elevated interrupts and queue drops. need to coordinate with infra to schedule firmware patch/testing on similar hardware.
Raj Patel: opening infra ticket INF-2026-982 to track firmware remediation plan. linking to customer RCA.
Ana Romero: action items before handing off to post-incident comms: 
- Devon: finish full pcap + dmesg upload (due 04:20 UTC)
- Raj: infra ticket + firmware roll plan (due 04:45 UTC)
- Samantha: finalize customer comm and exec brief (send update to Harper and AuroraCorp execs by 04:00 UTC)
- Ethan: run health sweep across other hosts in aurora pool to confirm no siblings affected (due 04:15 UTC)

Ana Romero: leaving notes in ticket and assigning follow-up meeting for RCA review at 2026-04-12 14:00 UTC. please add any items.
Devon Li: uploaded pcap and dmesg to the incident doc. marking mitigation stable. 5xx now <0.5% for 15m.
Harper (ACME) - CS: appreciate the quick updates, thanks everyone. please confirm final customer note when sent.
Samantha Chen: sent customer update to Harper and execs at 03:58 UTC. copy of sent comm in the incident doc. proposed SLA credit workflow: CS to approve credit, finance to apply credit to next invoice, and post an update here once applied.
Raj Patel: infra ticket INF-2026-982 created. ETA: firmware window provisioning next week; will propose candidate hosts for canary.
Ana Romero: RCA timeline confirmed: preliminary RCA draft in 48h, final RCA + remediation plan in 72h. cross-team postmortem meeting scheduled.
Devon Li: tagging follow-ups: `#action: collect-kernel-logs`, `#action: run-firmware-canary`, `#action: customer-comm-sent`. closing live mitigation — monitoring for 1hr then will move to post-incident channel.
Incident Bot: INCIDENT: AB-2026-418 marked as mitigated at 04:18 UTC by Devon Li. next step: post-incident review.
Samantha Chen: noted. Harper — we'll follow up with the RCA and the proposed credit application. closing this channel for live paging; follow-ups will be in the incident doc and the scheduled RCA meeting.
Harper (ACME) - CS: thanks all — appreciate the visibility.
Raj Patel: adding final note: if we see weird NIC events on similar SKUs, open immediate page; otherwise proceed with planned canary patch next week.
Ana Romero: thanks team. will hand off remaining tasks to oncall rotation and CS for billing/credits."

title: #incidents: 2487654321
preview:
Priya Nair: Quick async check on RCX-427 postmortem actions — want to lock owners + due dates so we can close the loop. Can owners please ack and confirm ETA? If you're blocked, call it out here.

Ethan Park: I can own the infra item (A1). ETA Wed 2026-03-25 but blocked on a quota increase request. @finance can you confirm the GPU quota bump? 

Marcus Li: I'll take the runbook update (A4). Targeting 2026-03-28. I can pair with eng-sre for validation.

Lena Ortiz: Taking runtime backport (A2). Plan is to ship by 2026-04-02, but I'll try to prioritize and push to 2026-03-29 if no regressions show up in canary.

Zoe Chen: QA will add the regression tests (A3). Need the failing prompt set + expected outputs from product to scope. ETA 2026-03-30 after I get the prompt set.

Priya Nair: Thanks — here's the compact action list I'm tracking:
```
A1 infra: increase node pool & config (owner: Ethan Park) — due 2026-03-25 
A2 runtime: backport fix + canary (owner: Lena Ortiz) — due 2026-04-02
A3 qa: regression suite + integration test (owner: Zoe Chen) — due 2026-03-30
A4 runbook: update cutover & rollback steps (owner: Marcus Li) — due 2026-03-28
```
Please paste any blockers inline so I can escalate. I'll update the tracker: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/RCX-427/actions

Ethan Park: noted. I've opened quota ticket with cloud (link: https://cloud.provider/req/9876). If that doesn't clear by EOD Mon I'll request temporary burst capacity. @Priya Nair please escalate to finance if needed. :thumbsup:

incident-bot: Task A1 created in RCX-427 actions (task id: t-9087)

Marcus Li: Yes, I can take that verification window. Suggest we schedule it Tue 10:00 PT — works for me.

Zoe Chen: Product folks — can someone drop the failing prompts here or in the tracker? I need them to finalize the test cases.

Priya Nair: @Evan (product) — can you drop the prompt set? If you can't, @Zoe Chen I can seed a minimal set from logs.

QA-bot: Regression pipeline created: pipeline/rcx-427-regress, will run on PRs and nightly.

Priya Nair: Perfect. I'll update the Jira actions with these confirmations and close once all tasks are marked Done. If any ETA slides, please update the ticket and ping this thread. Thanks all. :rocket:

title: #incidents: 2419001234
preview:
incident-bot: PagerDuty alert fired: PD#INC-4821 — auth-token-validate error rate > 5% in prod. Trigger: auth-validate service -> 'kid not found' (JWT). Created channel #incident-4821. Alert threshold breached at ~2026-03-14T11:03:00Z. Link: https://pagerduty.example/INC-4821 :rotating_light:

pagerduty: Alert details: service=auth-validate, env=prod, metric=token_validation_error_rate, value=7.8%, window=5m. Automatic page sent to oncall.

Alice Nguyen: Acked — I'm oncall and pulling this in. Triage starting.

Irene Sato: I see a flood of "kid not found" in auth-validate at the LB layer. Sample log:
```
[2026-03-14T11:02:58Z] auth-validate: warn request_id=abc123 uid=svc-xyz error="jwt: no matching key (kid 3e4f)" path=/v1/chat
[2026-03-14T11:02:59Z] auth-validate: warn request_id=def456 uid=svc-xyz error="jwt: no matching key (kid 3e4f)" path=/v1/generate
```
Possible cause: jwks cache not updated after key rotation. Checking jwks-cache + key-service.

Ben Carter: I'm awake, joining. Any key rotation events around 10:58?

Alice Nguyen: Yes, there was an automated key rotation at 2026-03-14T10:58:10Z (key-service/rotate job). It reports success but I see spikes of 502/504 from jwks-cache to key-service in the control plane logs.

Irene Sato: Running quick checks: 
`kubectl logs deployment/jwks-cache --since=10m | grep -E "error|timeout|504" | tail -n 20` shows repeated timeouts to key-service. Also `curl -s https://jwks-cache.prod.internal/.well-known/jwks.json | jq -c '.'` returns an empty object intermittently.

Irene Sato: Hypothesis: key-service rotated and returned new JWKS with new kid (3e4f) but jwks-cache instances didn't fetch it due to transient errors -> auth-validate attempting verification against cache sees missing kid -> fails.

Ben Carter: Mitigation options: (1) Force jwks-cache to bypass cache & fetch directly from key-service, (2) enable fallback acceptance of previous key in auth-validate for short window, (3) rollback key rotation (promote previous key) if propagation cannot be restored quickly.

Alice Nguyen: I'm going to flush jwks-cache instances first (lowest impact). Executing: `kubectl exec -n infra jwks-cache-0 -- curl -XPOST http://localhost:8080/flush` and same for -1/-2.

Irene Sato: Flushed jwks-cache across all pods, 11:12 UTC. Patch applied to auth-validate to accept multiple kids temporarily: `kubectl set env deployment/auth-validate ALLOW_FALLBACK_KIDS=true` (non-intrusive, no restart).

CS-ops: Noted. We're tracking affected customers: several mid-market tenants showing spikes in 401 on generate/chat. CS will prepare a proactive note once we have expected ETA.

pagerduty: Incident acknowledged by Alice Nguyen at 2026-03-14T11:13:04Z.

Irene Sato: After flush and env change, error rate dropped sharply. Metrics: token_validation_error_rate -> 0.9% in next 5m (was 7.8%). Logs now show both old and new kids present in jwks-cache.

Ben Carter: Good. Root cause likely a transient failure path in jwks-cache where retries/backoff caused a brief window with empty keys. Follow-ups: add stricter monitoring on jwks-cache fetch latency, add golden-record sanity check after rotations, and add immediate cache invalidation hooks post-rotation.

Alice Nguyen: Doing handoff to Ben Carter for 12:00-14:00 UTC coverage while I compile timeline and customer impact. Please document any changes; we've already set ALLOW_FALLBACK_KIDS=true — we'll remove that once healthy for 1 hour.

incident-bot: On-call handover: Alice Nguyen -> Ben Carter at 2026-03-14T12:00:00Z.

Ben Carter: Monitoring. Observed sustained error rate <1% for 20m. Will keep open until 13:00 UTC and then close if stable. Mitigation timeline:
- 11:03 detect
- 11:10 triage
- 11:12 cache flush + fallback enabled
- 11:20 error rate back to normal

CS-ops: Drafted outbound note to customers, marking incident as "service interruption (auth token validation) affecting API traffic". ETA for full postmortem: EOD tomorrow.

Irene Sato: Post-incident tasks (adding to Jira):
- AUTH-928: add immediate cache invalidation webhook to key-service (priority P0)
- AUTH-929: strengthen jwks-cache retry/backoff to prefer short-circuit on rotation events (P1)
- AUTH-930: add canary checks after rotation that verify keys exist in all jwks-cache replicas (P1)

Ben Carter: I'll schedule a follow-up incident review at 2026-03-15T09:30Z to go over telemetry and rollout changes.

Alice Nguyen: Closing notes: temporary mitigation ALLOW_FALLBACK_KIDS=true will be reverted at 2026-03-14T13:15Z unless we see regressions. Final root cause suspected: transient network/503s from control-plane -> jwks-cache missed a rotation window.

incident-bot: Incident status update: resolved (mitigated) at 2026-03-14T11:23:17Z by Ben Carter.

Ben Carter: Confirmed stable for 60m. Marking incident resolved and will own postmortem. Good job team :thumbsup:

Alice Nguyen: Thanks all. Will attach logs, graphs, and exact commands to the Jira and postmortem doc.

CS-ops: Communicated to customers at 11:45 UTC. No escalations so far.

Irene Sato: Leaving final monitoring watch for another hour then archive the channel.

Ben Carter: archiving at 12:24 UTC if no more alerts.

incident-bot: Channel archived."

title: #incidents: 2381234567
preview:
IncidentsBot: :rotating_light: Alert: authz-service > error-rate threshold (token-verification failures). Suspected upstream Okta JWKS origin latency. Paging @Priya Shah (on-call) — please acknowledge.
Priya Shah: acked, looking. Which services are showing errors?
Alice Nguyen: seeing a spike in verifier errors across auth-proxy and console routes. Example logs from verifier:
Alice Nguyen: ```ERROR verifier: signature verification failed: key not found (kid: abc123) - upstream jwks fetch status=504 time=1200ms``` 
Alice Nguyen: Console 403s start ~2381234500, token-introspect calls to Okta are timing out or returning 5xx.
Samir Patel: I'm hitting Okta JWKS endpoint directly: `curl -i https://company.okta.com/oauth2/v1/keys` -> intermittent 504s and long TTFB (~1.3s). Possibly origin rate-limiting during morning sync.
Ben Carter: Confirmed. Our jwks cache miss + synchronous fetch path is blowing up under thundering herd from many short-lived tokens. Cache TTLs are low (30s). We need immediate mitigation to avoid console-wide login failures.
Priya Shah: Mitigation options?
Ben Carter: Fast mitigations in order:
- bump local jwks cache TTL to 5m
- enable verifier local-key-fallback (use last-good JWKS from disk instead of failing)
- change console auth route to prefer session cookies and skip remote introspect for browser flows (falls back to our existing session validator)
Ben Carter: I can push a quick roll (config change) for cache TTL and toggle fallback. Command:
Ben Carter: ```
# set feature flag
curl -X POST https://config.internal/api/flags -H 'Authorization: Bearer $OP' -d '{"flag":"auth.okta_local_fallback","value":true}'
# update cache TTL
curl -X POST https://config.internal/api/services/auth-proxy -d '{"jwks_cache_ttl":300}'
``` 
Priya Shah: Do it. Also add a temporary route weight to prefer dedicated auth-delta instances (less load) for console.
Ben Carter: applying config now. Pushing flag -> :white_check_mark:
Ben Carter: TTL updated -> :white_check_mark:
Marcus Lee: After the TTL bump, I see jwks fetch rate drop and error-rate decreasing. Verifier still failing for some tokens with kid mismatch; last-good-jwks fallback served a lot of verifications.
Alice Nguyen: Observability note: increased 5xx from Okta between 2381234480-2381234560, peak at 2381234522. Console 403s aligned. Starting to see gradual recovery in the last 30s.
Elena Morales: From support: ~12 unique customers reported SSO/console login issues, highest impact for customers in EU region. Have we drafted a short status page update?
Priya Shah: Yes. Suggested status message: "We are experiencing degraded login service for some customers due to intermittent issues with our auth provider. We have applied mitigations and are monitoring. Some users may need to re-login. ETA to full recovery TBD." Want me to publish?
Elena Morales: Please post. I'll coordinate outbound comms for active customers.
Samir Patel: For longer-term: implement jittered refresh + asynchronous background JWKS refresh to avoid synchronous origin fetch on cache miss. Also consider HSM-signed fallback keys for emergency verification.
Ben Carter: Agreed. I'll open follow-ups: #1 async jwks refresh, #2 persist last-good-jwks to disk on every successful fetch, #3 increase default TTL and add jitter. 
Priya Shah: Handoff: I'm staying on incident until stable. Marcus, can you take on next 45m monitoring shift? I'll update pager and escalate to SRE lead if errors creep back up.
Marcus Lee: taking it. I'll watch error-rate and alert if >3% for 5m.
Priya Shah: Thanks — publishing status now. :thumbsup:
IncidentsBot: Status update posted to status.redwood.ai (incident-2381234567). Ticket created: INC-2026-238
Elena Morales: I'll send an email to affected customers with the status link and a short description.
Alice Nguyen: Postmortem items captured: root cause likely Okta origin saturation plus low TTL + synchronous fetch path. Remediation planned as Ben listed. Will draft RCA after incident window closes.
Priya Shah: last check -> error rates steady and console logins recovering. Marking incident as monitoring. Marcus, escalate if regression.
Marcus Lee: acknowledged.

Alice Nguyen: Postmortem items captured: root cause likely Okta origin saturation plus low TTL + synchronous fetch path. Remediation planned as Ben listed. Will draft RCA after incident window closes.
Priya Shah: last check -> error rates steady and console logins recovering. Marking incident as monitoring. Marcus, escalate if regression.
Marcus Lee: acknowledged.

title: #incidents: 2219993330
preview:
Marta Liu: Quick roundup from the monthly pattern scan  I pulled the top repeating failure signatures across prod for the last quarter. Posting short list + proposed owners so we can close outstanding postmortem actions.

Marta Liu: Major recurring signatures:
- cold GPU warmup causing 95th+ latency spikes during scale-up
- KV/prefix rebuild storms that trigger extra token churn and retries
- autoscaler oscillation in the low-util noisy-worker pool
- rare hangs in quantized long-context decodes
- auth rate-limit surges for a small customer cohort

Ethan Park: Can we split into 2-week remediation sprints? I'll take the autoscaler oscillation. Short plan: increase cool-down window, add oscillation circuit-breaker metric, and run a chaos scale test. ETA: config PR + test plan by next Wed (2219993490).

Sofia Alvarez: I'll own KV/prefix rebuilds. Plan: build synthetic prefix traffic, tighten locking on rebuild path, and prototype an interim stale-serving fallback behind a feature flag. ETA: repro + flag RFC by next Fri (2219993620).

Marta Liu: For GPU warmup, does it look driver-related or purely load timing?

Ethan Park: Early signal leans to cold-start timing; a subset of nodes with older drivers show worse tails. I'll produce a driver correlation table.

Marta Liu: Good. If warmup looks driver-linked, we can either rollback images or add a lightweight preload step on boot.

DevOpsBot: Scheduled run: "scale-stress-run" tomorrow 03:00 UTC on pool noisy-worker for autoscaler tests.

Sofia Alvarez: I'll coordinate with DevOps for a paired run to repro the KV storm while autoscaler test is active.

IncidentBot: Created ticket INC-3122 "Autoscaler oscillation - recurring" and INC-3123 "KV prefix rebuild - recurring" and linked to this thread.

Marta Liu: Actions summary:
- Ethan: INC-3122 autoscaler config + chaos test (ETA next Wed).
- Sofia: INC-3123 repro + stale-serving flag RFC (ETA next Fri).
- Marta/Ethan: GPU warmup driver correlation and proposed mitigation (ETA EOW).

Marta Liu: We'll have a 20-min sync next Thursday to confirm artifacts and owners; drop updates here if anything slips. :calendar:

title: #incidents: 2187654321
preview:
Alice Nguyen: Heads up — I've been tracking the DB connection incidents (3 in ~6 weeks). Before we lock the postmortem fixes, we should align on customer-facing comms and whether we change RCA-sharing policy. Quick sync?

Ben Carter: +1. We should also decide if recurring patterns get different comms cadence. Any consolidated pattern notes yet?

Priya Shah: Pulled a short summary: https://docs.redwood/pm/INC-DBCONN-summary — pattern: brief spikes during batch windows, similar failure signature each time. I flagged common traces in the doc. :eyes:

Marco Ruiz: The pattern looks actionable. For statuspage language I recommend we move from generic "service degraded" to something like "intermittent connectivity affecting a subset of requests" — more specific without being alarmist. Thoughts?

Elena Park: Agree on specificity. Also we need a clear rule for what we share in public RCAs vs private customer RCAs (redacted vs full). Last time we over-redacted and customers asked for more detail.

Alice Nguyen: Proposed checklist to decide now:
1) Statuspage phrasing update (ops to draft new sentence)
2) 72-hour customer summary template (short, non-technical) with optional detailed 7-day RCA for affected customers
3) Policy for sharing full RCAs: provide on request with consent + legal review for non-Enterprise accounts
Any objections?

Priya Shah: No objections. Action plan suggestion:
- Priya: draft statuspage sentence + 72h template by EOD Tue
- Marco: run deeper pattern analysis, attach mitigation recommendations by Fri
- Ben: update comms playbook with consent flow & tags by next Mon
- Elena: produce redaction checklist and security sign-off timeline (target Thu)
I can create the tracker tasks now.

incident-bot: Created follow-up tasks: #INC-DB-1001 (statuspage draft) assigned to Priya Shah, due 2187710000; #INC-DB-1002 (pattern analysis) assigned to Marco Ruiz, due 2187752000; #INC-DB-1003 (comms-playbook update) assigned to Ben Carter, due 2187810000.

Ben Carter: I'll also add a "recurring-pattern" tag to related postmortems and add an automated weekly dashboard. Proposal: if we see >=2 similar incidents within 30 days, trigger auto-escalation to Eng-Platform and Product for a prioritized mitigation ticket.

Marco Ruiz: Sounds good. I'll include detection thresholds and sample queries in the pattern analysis. Also proposing one-line summary snippets for the statuspage and for the 72h note. Example draft (for ops to tweak):

```
Proposed statuspage blurb: "We experienced intermittent connection failures in our inference layer affecting a subset of requests. We've deployed mitigations and are monitoring."
```

Elena Park: Security is okay with the high-level blurb. For any full RCA requested by a customer we require: (a) redaction checklist applied, (b) legal approval if customer requests unredacted logs, (c) support to record explicit consent. I'll post the checklist by Thu.

Alice Nguyen: Good. For public-facing timeline: short email/update in 72h and an internal detailed 7-day RCA. If affected customers request more, route to Support + Legal and then share under consent.

Priya Shah: I'll draft the 72h template and put it in #product-comms. Quick template idea: 1) What happened (plain language), 2) Impact (who/what), 3) Mitigation taken, 4) Next steps + ETA. Keep it <= 3 bullets.

Ben Carter: Owners and ETAs recap: Priya (statuspage + 72h) by Tue EOD, Marco (pattern analysis) by Fri, Ben (playbook + tags) by next Mon, Elena (security redaction) by Thu, Alice (customer outreach coordination) monitors. Internal playbook update by next Mon; public statuspage text aimed for Wed.

Marco Ruiz: Do we want a short follow-up sync to confirm wording and playbook edits? 30m tomorrow works for me.

Priya Shah: I'm free 10–11am PT.

Ben Carter: 11–12 PT works for me.

Alice Nguyen: Let's do 11:00am PT — I'll send a calendar invite and add the agenda: 1) statuspage text, 2) 72h template, 3) consent flow steps, 4) pattern detection thresholds.

incident-bot: Calendar invite created: "Pattern-informed comms playbook review" — 11:00 PT tomorrow.

Alice Nguyen: Thanks all — please keep updates in the tracker and tag me if anything slips. I'll consolidate final wording after the sync. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 2163456789
preview:
SRE Bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: dedicated-acme-eu pool GPU_mem_pressure >= 95% (5m) and queued_requests > 200; paging oncall.
Alex Chen: noticed a slow ramp starting ~09:02 UTC, sustained for 8m. Embedding refresh + nightly indexer for ACME kicking off. GPUs reporting high mem and kernel retries.
Maria Gf3mez: grafana snapshot: 6/6 nodes > 93% GPU_mem, avg latency spiking 3x, errors show `CUDA OOM` on a few worker pods.
Timothy Park: I'm oncall. Quick question: can we free any tenant-local state (KV caches, prefix cache) without infra restart?
Alex Chen: yes. We have a runtime command to evict tenant caches. Running `infractl cache evict --tenant=acme --scope=prefix:* --confirm` now (will affect warm starts).
Infra Admin Bot: cache-evict job accepted (job id: evict-9861). ETA ~45s.
Jordan Blake: Heads up: ACME's indexer is in their control plane; account team on a call says they can pause it for 30m if needed. They prefer we try in-place first.
Alex Chen: cache evict completed. Observed ~4-8% reduction in mem across nodes. Not enough alone.
Maria Gf3mez: autoscaler log shows failed scale due to ENI quota for VPC subnet (attach limit):
```
[2163456798] autoscaler: scale_request: +3 nodes
[2163456798] k8s-cloud: attach_network_interface failed: eni_limit_exceeded
```
Maria Gf3mez: also a protection flag blocked cross-region spill: `pool-policy: disallow-cross-region-burst=true` set by ops.
Timothy Park: options checklist: a) pause ACME indexer (fast) b) enable cross-region spill to dedicated-acme-us (adds latency) c) emergency burst toggle after infra approval (adds capacity in-region) d) force model fallback to lower-memory variants. Which first?
Jordan Blake: ACME ok with short pause to indexer if it solves it immediately. They want to avoid cross-region routing if possible.
Elaine Rivera: Account approves indexer pause and will sign emergency billing for burst if needed. Legal cleared.
Alex Chen: executing pause: `acmectl job pause --tenant acme --job indexer --reason "emergency capacity"`
Alex Chen: indexer job paused. Monitoring...
Maria Gf3mez: good drop in queue length. GPU_mem down ~6% more. Latency improving but still above normal.
Timothy Park: still seeing kernel retries for chat endpoints. Let's enable model fallback for embedding endpoints to quantized variant: `rictl model set-variant --tenant acme --endpoint /v1/embeddings --variant embed-q4_0 --rollout 100%`
Alex Chen: applied. Also lowering per-tenant streaming concurrency cap to 170: `rictl router set-policy --tenant acme --max-concurrency 170` to protect other tenants.
Infra Admin Bot: alert: infra-admins required to toggle `disallow-cross-region-burst`. Would you like me to page infra-admins?
Timothy Park: Page infra-admins. We may still need cross-region if mem keeps rebounding.
Infra Admin Bot: paging infra-admins group now.
Alex Chen: meanwhile I'm going to attempt a hot-swap of a few large KV caches to disk to free GPU host memory: `infractl kv-offload --tenant acme --threshold 500MB` (starting).
Maria Gf3mez: metrics: after cache offload + indexer pause + model fallback, GPU_mem at ~72% across nodes. Error rate dropped to baseline.
Jordan Blake: ACME confirms their dashboard shows errors cleared. They still want us to request quota increases to avoid repetition.
Elaine Rivera: I've got approval for a 3-hour emergency cross-region shift if infra requests it; billable. But prefer not to if current fixes hold.
Infra Admin Bot: infra-admin Alice accepted the page: investigating ENI quota and pool policy.
Alex Chen: proposing follow-ups: 1) quota increase request to cloud ops for ENIs & vCPUs 2) add per-tenant background-job soft-limits 3) add an automatic model-variant fallback when GPU_mem > 80% for >3m 4) one-click emergency burst toggle for pool owners.
Timothy Park: taking over monitoring. I'll keep burst options on standby. Handing to me for 45m watch.
SRE Bot: Incident acknowledged by oncall (Timothy Park). Severity: Sev2 (major customer degraded). Current status: mitigated (indexer paused + model fallback + cache offload). ETA to close: monitor 45m, then post-incident review.
Alex Chen: creating incident ticket INC-216345 and attaching graphs + runbook steps executed.
Jordan Blake: I'll prepare a short customer note summarizing actions and optional next steps (quota request + scheduled maintenance window).
Maria Gf3mez: adding action: implement per-tenant enqueue throttle for embeddings.
Timothy Park: will update thread if anything flares. For now, mitigations effective. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 2145684321
preview:
Infra-Bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: spike in 5xx responses for ingress in apac1c (avg 16% over 90s). Severity: P1. Dashboard: https://grafana.example/ingress/apac1c. Runbook: https://confluence.example/runbooks/ingress-failures

Ava Chen: oncall here, acknowledged. Pulling logs + target health.

Ravi Patel: seeing lots of 502/503 and upstream `connection reset by peer`. Start time around 2145684200. Any recent infra changes?

Oncall-Relay: paging Ava Chen (oncall).

Leo Martins: checked envoy control plane logs from ingress pods in apac1c: repeated `upstream reset: connection reset`, and TLS errors: `tls: unsupported protocol`.

NetOps: we pushed a Route53 canary to shift 20% traffic to apac1c about 6 minutes ago (change ref R53-APAC-CANARY-2026-03-15).

Support-Escalations: customers reporting errors broadly; we have ~900 failed requests aggregated across a handful of tenants. Enterprise customers starting to open tickets.

Ava Chen: mitigation plan: 1) revert R53 canary to previous weights, 2) scale ingress controller to reduce per-instance load, 3) revert recent EnvoyFilter changes if config looks suspect. Who can flip Route53?

NetOps: I can revert R53 weights. Preparing change batch; lowering TTL to 20s for faster propagation.

Ravi Patel: grabbing pod logs and target group health checks:
```
kubectl -n ingress get pods -l app=envoy-ingress -o wide
kubectl -n ingress logs envoy-ingress-7c9f -c envoy --tail=300 | egrep "reset|tls|handshake"
aws elbv2 describe-target-health --target-group-arn arn:aws:elasticloadbalancing:apac:tg/12345
```

Leo Martins: there was an EnvoyFilter change merged ~4m before alerts from CI canary. It rewrites incoming path headers and injects a JWT filter. Backend teams flagged intermittent TLS negotiation failures in staging yesterday.

NetOps: Route53 change submitted to shift 95% traffic back to apac1a (change ID R53-ROLLBACK-214568).

Infra-Bot: Route53 rollback executed by NetOps at 2145684423. Notifying incident channel.

Ava Chen: scaling envoy ingress replicas to 7 in apac1c to reduce connection churn.
```
kubectl -n ingress scale deployment envoy-ingress --replicas=7
```

Ravi Patel: after R53 rollback error rate fell but still seeing RSTs. Capturing packet traces for resets:
```
kubectl exec -n ingress envoy-ingress-7c9f -- tcpdump -i any 'tcp[tcpflags] & tcp-rst != 0' -w /tmp/rst.pcap
kubectl cp ingress/envoy-ingress-7c9f:/tmp/rst.pcap ./rst.pcap
```

Leo Martins: PCAPs show upstream ALB sending TCP RST shortly after the TLS ClientHello. Looks like ALB rejecting the TLS handshake or marking target unhealthy. Also seeing healthcheck 503s on the target group.

NetOps: inspecting ALB listener and target group. Listener TLS policy `ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS-1-2-2021-06` unchanged. Target group healthchecks hitting `/healthz` but EnvoyFilter change rewrote paths to `/internal/health` for the proxy, so ALB health checks started failing -> targets drained/unhealthy -> connection resets.

Ava Chen: that points to config regression. Roll back the EnvoyFilter change now. Ravi, can you apply previous manifest?

Ravi Patel: rolling back EnvoyFilter at 2145684550 with `kubectl apply -f envoyfilter-prev.yaml`.

Support-Escalations: customers want an ETA and summary. We should estimate impacted window and number of failed requests.

Oncall-Relay: handoff: Leo taking lead on mitigation starting 2145684560. Ava remains incident commander.

Leo Martins: EnvoyFilter revert applied at 2145684560. Restored healthcheck passthrough and header behavior.

Infra-Bot: automated target-group check: healthy targets increased 3 -> 8 within 45s after rollback.

Ava Chen: monitoring: 5xx rate dropped to ~0.4% now (baseline ~0.15%). Latency normalizing. Customers report successful retries.

NetOps: restoring Route53 weights to standard now that backends healthy. Change ID R53-RESTORE-214568.

Ravi Patel: collecting artifacts for postmortem: envoy logs, pcaps, ALB target health snapshots, Route53 change history, and the CI commit that introduced the EnvoyFilter. Bundling into /incidents/2145684321/artifacts.tar.gz

Support-Escalations: draft customer note: impacted window 2145684200 - 2145684680 (~8min). ~900 failed requests across 5 tenants, peak error rate ~16%, primarily apac1c. Preparing formal comms and SLA impact calc.

Leo Martins: preliminary root cause: faulty EnvoyFilter (deployed via CI canary) rewrote healthcheck paths and headers -> ALB healthchecks failed -> targets marked unhealthy -> ALB emitted RSTs to active connections -> clients saw 5xx and connection resets. Amplified by Route53 canary directing extra traffic to apac1c.

Ava Chen: action items and owners: 1) Incident postmortem (Ava + Leo) due EOD tomorrow, 2) CI gating + unit tests for EnvoyFilter changes (Ravi), 3) Update DNS canary runbook to include quick rollback steps (NetOps), 4) Customer comms + SLA assessment (Support-Escalations).

Support-Escalations: requested initial customer update in 20m.

Leo Martins: opening follow-up ticket INF-3478 to add synthetic healthcheck tests and block EnvoyFilter merges without a staging healthcheck pass.

Ava Chen: incident mitigated and severity downgraded to P2 at 2145684800. Thanks everyone, please add notes to the postmortem channel when you begin your writeups. :white_check_mark:
