Ava Chen: scaling envoy ingress replicas to 7 in apac1c to reduce connection churn.
```
kubectl -n ingress scale deployment envoy-ingress --replicas=7
```

Ravi Patel: after R53 rollback error rate fell but still seeing RSTs. Capturing packet traces for resets:
```
kubectl exec -n ingress envoy-ingress-7c9f -- tcpdump -i any 'tcp[tcpflags] & tcp-rst != 0' -w /tmp/rst.pcap
kubectl cp ingress/envoy-ingress-7c9f:/tmp/rst.pcap ./rst.pcap
```

Leo Martins: PCAPs show upstream ALB sending TCP RST shortly after the TLS ClientHello. Looks like ALB rejecting the TLS handshake or marking target unhealthy. Also seeing healthcheck 503s on the target group.

NetOps: inspecting ALB listener and target group. Listener TLS policy `ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS-1-2-2021-06` unchanged. Target group healthchecks hitting `/healthz` but EnvoyFilter change rewrote paths to `/internal/health` for the proxy, so ALB health checks started failing -> targets drained/unhealthy -> connection resets.

Ava Chen: that points to config regression. Roll back the EnvoyFilter change now. Ravi, can you apply previous manifest?

Ravi Patel: rolling back EnvoyFilter at 2145684550 with `kubectl apply -f envoyfilter-prev.yaml`.

Support-Escalations: customers want an ETA and summary. We should estimate impacted window and number of failed requests.

Oncall-Relay: handoff: Leo taking lead on mitigation starting 2145684560. Ava remains incident commander.

Leo Martins: EnvoyFilter revert applied at 2145684560. Restored healthcheck passthrough and header behavior.

Infra-Bot: automated target-group check: healthy targets increased 3 -> 8 within 45s after rollback.

Ava Chen: monitoring: 5xx rate dropped to ~0.4% now (baseline ~0.15%). Latency normalizing. Customers report successful retries.

NetOps: restoring Route53 weights to standard now that backends healthy. Change ID R53-RESTORE-214568.

Ravi Patel: collecting artifacts for postmortem: envoy logs, pcaps, ALB target health snapshots, Route53 change history, and the CI commit that introduced the EnvoyFilter. Bundling into /incidents/2145684321/artifacts.tar.gz

Support-Escalations: draft customer note: impacted window 2145684200 - 2145684680 (~8min). ~900 failed requests across 5 tenants, peak error rate ~16%, primarily apac1c. Preparing formal comms and SLA impact calc.

Leo Martins: preliminary root cause: faulty EnvoyFilter (deployed via CI canary) rewrote healthcheck paths and headers -> ALB healthchecks failed -> targets marked unhealthy -> ALB emitted RSTs to active connections -> clients saw 5xx and connection resets. Amplified by Route53 canary directing extra traffic to apac1c.

Ava Chen: action items and owners: 1) Incident postmortem (Ava + Leo) due EOD tomorrow, 2) CI gating + unit tests for EnvoyFilter changes (Ravi), 3) Update DNS canary runbook to include quick rollback steps (NetOps), 4) Customer comms + SLA assessment (Support-Escalations).

Support-Escalations: requested initial customer update in 20m.

Leo Martins: opening follow-up ticket INF-3478 to add synthetic healthcheck tests and block EnvoyFilter merges without a staging healthcheck pass.

Ava Chen: incident mitigated and severity downgraded to P2 at 2145684800. Thanks everyone, please add notes to the postmortem channel when you begin your writeups. :white_check_mark:

title: #incidents: 2051234567
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty alert fired: service=apse1-az2-serving severity=high reason=cpu AND queue_latency spike. PD incident link: https://pagerduty.example/incidents/PD-83421 - automated page to oncall

pagerduty: Incident PD-83421 auto-assigned to Maya Chen (primary oncall). Escalation schedule in effect.

Maya Chen: acked PD-83421 :white_check_mark: I'm looking. Observability shows 95th CPU at 98% on ae-worker-03 and request queue growing.

Luis Ortega: seeing a lot of GRPC retry loops from routing-proxy in az2. kubeeviction rate high for the inference pods?

Maya Chen: checking node metrics now. `kubectl get pods -n redwood-serving -o wide --selector=zone=az2` shows a few pods in CrashLoopBackoff and multiple pods at OOMKilled.

Priya Nair: any live customer impact? Claire (Support) do we have tickets or chats flagging errors?

Claire (Support): a handful of 500s from eu-customer-42 and one enterprise tenant hitting timeouts on generation calls. Support flagged priority-to-sales for the enterprise one.

Ben Carter: logs show repeated `grpc: error: code = ResourceExhausted desc = KV cache eviction` from runtime. Looks like K/V cache GC thrashing after memory pressure.

Maya Chen: hypothesis: sudden shard imbalance + node flapping leading to high cold-cache ops -> memory spike -> evictions -> more cold cache. Initiating mitigation: scale up reserved pool in az2 and activate cross-region fallback.

Maya Chen: running command to bump pool: ```
# scale dedicated pool in az2
infractl pool scale --name apse1-az2-serving --replicas 6
```

deploy-bot: Received scale request by Maya Chen. Scaling apse1-az2-serving to 6 replicas. Progress: 2/6 ready.

Luis Ortega: while autoscaler spins up new replicas we should enable routing fallback to apse1-az1 and eu-west-1 for new requests. Adding routing rule now.

Maya Chen: enabling fallback policy (weight-based) - only for non-sticky sessions and new requests. Command: ```
redwoodctl route update --service apse1-serve --fallback "apse1-az1:0.5,eu-west-1:0.5" --conditions "region==apse1 AND zone==az2 AND latency>200ms"
```

deploy-bot: Routing policy updated. New requests from az2 will be routed to fallback pool per rule.

Priya Nair: notify Observability and customer success. I'll draft status for the incident channel and customers. Claire please collect affected request IDs and tenant IDs.

Claire (Support): collecting now. Two customers with elevated error rates (tenant ids: T-3385, T-7761). Will open interim status emails.

Maya Chen: PD-83421: ack still active. I'm triggering canary failover for low-priority traffic to eu-west-1 to verify model compatibility. Model variant: `redwood-open-gpt-medium-quant` (compat-fallback).

Ben Carter: note: quantized variant has ~5-10% perf delta on some prompts. We'll monitor quality metrics.

Luis Ortega: adding temporary token bucket to limit bursty batch producers in az2 to reduce pressure: ```
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: az2-burst-control
data:
  max_inflight: "150"
EOF
```

Maya Chen: good. Observability: queue length starting to drop after routing update. CPU on ae-worker-03 down from 98% -> 72% in last 4m. New pods coming up.

pagerduty: Reminder: if incident not acknowledged in 15m escalate to Luis Ortega (secondary).

Maya Chen: I'm handing off active management to Luis for stabilization and postmortem. Luis, I'm leaving notes in the incident runbook (see /incidents/runbooks/apse1-az2-memory).

Luis Ortega: taking over. I'll keep PD updated and coordinate rollback if quality regression observed.

Priya Nair: quick customer note: "We've identified a regional serving degradation in ap-southeast-1 (az2). We've routed new requests to fallback pools; affected customers may see slightly different latency or model version. We're monitoring quality and will provide updates every 15 minutes." Approve?

Maya Chen: approved, send. Add mention of specific tenant impact T-3385/T-7761.

Claire (Support): status message sent to affected customers and flagged escalation to account team for T-7761.

Ben Carter: monitoring for regressions: running a quick eval set against fallback model. Initial diffs show 97% parity — acceptable for emergency routing.

Luis Ortega: OK. plan: 1) let scaled nodes stabilize 2) monitor KV cache hit ratio and GC 3) if stable after 30m, restore normal routing and drain fallback 4) postmortem to cover shard balancing + autoscaler tuning.

Maya Chen: adding timeline to runbook and creating Jira EPIC: INF-8239 (postmortem + mitigations).

deploy-bot: Scale complete: 6/6 replicas ready for apse1-az2-serving.

Maya Chen: great — begin draining fallback after we see 10m stable metrics.

Luis Ortega: marking PD-83421 as under-control, will set to monitored.

pagerduty: PD-83421 status changed to "under_control" by Luis Ortega.

Maya Chen: follow-up actions for postmortem: - investigate autoscaler thresholds and OOM patterns - add guardrails for KV cache burst - improve shard rebalancer to avoid hotspots - consider proactive warm-up when autoscaling events predicted

Priya Nair: I'll coordinate customer follow-ups and update the incident summary. Next update in 15 minutes unless anything changes. :hourglass_flowing_sand:

title: #incidents: 1992456789
preview:
Ethan Park: Follow-up thread for the outage postmortem — we need to lock remediation owners and decide public-facing text for the status page and a canned response for customers who ask for an RCA. Also: how do we handle recurring incidents policy-wise?

Mira Patel: Owners were discussed in the pm: TTL enforcement -> @Leah, autoscale policy -> @Nikhil, rollout gating -> infra. We should put hard due dates and owner contact info in the action tracker.

Leah Chen: I can take TTL enforcement. The root cause is a legacy deploy hook that resets cache TTL to 0 on some deploys. I want to add a config guard and a unit test to prevent regressions. Estimate 6 business days — target 2026-04-04.

Nikhil Rao: Autoscale fix is a missed anomaly threshold when bursts come from multi-region fanout. I'll add an adaptive threshold and a pre-scaling warmup. 4 business days. Target 2026-03-31.

Mira Patel: Good. Ops-bot please create tasks and assign owners with the due dates.

ops-bot: Task created: "Enforce cache TTL guard" - assignee: Leah Chen - due 1992662400. Task created: "Adaptive autoscaling thresholds" - assignee: Nikhil Rao - due 1992441600.

Ethan Park: On comms — CS wants transparent but concise language. Proposal: short status page entry within 12 hours with a link to an "RCA summary" (non-sensitive, redacted). Full internal RCA will remain internal; we will provide an expanded summary only on request and after a review by Legal if it contains privileged data. Thoughts?

Leah Chen: Agree. We should avoid sharing timelines of internal mitigation steps in public copy. For customers with compliance needs, we can provide a sanitized summary under NDA.

Mira Patel: Draft status page blurb (please edit):

```
Incident: Increased latency on hosted inference API
Status: Resolved
Summary: A deployment script unintentionally cleared cache TTLs under burst traffic, increasing compute and latency for a minority of requests (~4% of requests during the window). Mitigation: We reverted the change and applied an emergency guard to stabilize latency.
Next steps: Implement TTL enforcement, update autoscaling thresholds, and add rollout gating. Customers may request a redacted RCA summary; full internal logs remain confidential.
```

Nikhil Rao: Looks good. Suggest changing "minority" to the percent if metrics confirm ~4%. Also we should link to a short FAQ explaining what "redacted RCA" contains.

Customer Success Bot: Note: CS requests a pre-approved email template for customers who ask. Please include: impact window, percent of traffic affected, mitigation, ETA for permanent fix, and contact for follow-up.

Ethan Park: Draft email template in thread: 

"Hi <Customer>,

During <time window> we saw elevated latency affecting ~4% of requests to the hosted inference API. We reverted a configuration change and applied a guard to stabilize latency. We are implementing a permanent TTL enforcement and autoscale improvements. ETA for fixes: see action tracker (link). If you would like a redacted RCA summary, reply and we'll share one under our standard doc-sharing process.

Regards,
Redwood Support"

Mira Patel: Policy question: do we proactively publish RCA summaries for all customers or only on request? My vote: publish a brief redacted summary link on the status page for transparency, but do not publish internal runbooks or logs.

Leah Chen: Agrees with redacted summary on status page. It helps reduce inbound CS load.

Nikhil Rao: Fine with redacted summary. For recurring incidents, recommend a "recurrence review" after 30 days if a root cause tag has occurred >2 times in 6 months. Add to postmortem process.

Ethan Park: I'll add a policy note to the postmortem: "Publish redacted RCA summary on status page within 48 hours; provide expanded sanitized RCA to customers on request; full internal RCA only for legal/regulatory requests after review."

Mira Patel: Can someone volunteer to draft the redacted RCA summary? Maybe Leah can draft technical portion and CS can draft the customer-facing intro.

Leah Chen: I can draft the technical blurb.

ops-bot: Reminder: Checkpoint meeting scheduled 2026-04-06 10:00 UTC to review fixes and recurrence dashboard.

Ethan Park: Action items recap: 1) Leah: TTL guard by 2026-04-04, 2) Nikhil: autoscale thresholds by 2026-03-31, 3) Publish redacted RCA summary draft by 2026-03-28, 4) Add recurrence review policy to postmortem process. I'll update the tracker and ping Legal for the sharing policy wording.

Mira Patel: Thanks everyone. I'll hold the status page publish until CS confirms the percent metric and the redacted summary is ready. :white_check_mark:

title: #incidents: 1977012345
preview:
incident-bot: INCIDENT DETECTED — prefix cache miss rate jumped 7x for prod-cluster-usw2 at 14:05 UTC; threshold breached. Paging oncall. :rotating_light:
Ava Chen: I'm on it, got the page. Looking at dashboards now (console -> /dash/cache/hitrate).
Leo Patel: seeing huge tail of misses on model-prefix 'rw-prod-*' across nodes, hitrate down from 92% -> 18% in 6m. Linking logs: https://logs.redwood.example/prod/stream?query=prefix_miss
Emily S.: any recent deploys?
Ava Chen: Nope, no code deploy in last 3 hours. There was a config push to cache-invalidator at 13:58 (infra-cfg-2026-03-15) that touched prefix TTLs.
Julian M.: I can check invalidation job timeline. Looks like the invalidator kicked a full-scan at 14:01 (job-id: inv-7801).
SRE-Ryan: paging handoff in 10m — adding myself as secondary. Quick mitigation: can we pause invalidator?
Ava Chen: Trying to find pause endpoint. inv-service has /admin/scan/pause but needs admin token. Running: `kubectl exec -n infra deploy/inv-service -- curl -sS -X POST http://localhost:8080/admin/scan/pause`
Leo Patel: Logs from inv-service show a flood of delete events for prefixes matching `rw-prod-*` starting 14:00:05. snippet:
```
2026-03-15T14:00:05Z INFO invalidator deleting prefix rw-prod-abc123
2026-03-15T14:00:06Z INFO invalidator deleting prefix rw-prod-xyz789
... (thousands)
```
Ava Chen: Executed pause, response 200. Confirmed job stopped; however hitrate still low — many requests now missing and being recomputed from origin, causing latency and customer errors.
Emily S.: Customer impact check: error budget alert shows 5s P95 latency spiked; error tracker flagged 6 customers with degraded responses (IDs: cust-821, cust-914...). Support already got inbound tickets.
Julian M.: hypothesis 1: invalidator purge was too aggressive due to wildcard misconfig (bad regex), evicted warm prefixes. hypothesis 2: TTL mis-sync across cache tiers causing faster expiry + invalidator scan coincident.
SRE-Ryan: Important: are we seeing cache-populate/backfill traffic? If backfilling is slow it explains sustained miss rate.
Ava Chen: Backfill logs show worker pool is overwhelmed; workers stuck on heavy origin requests (long tail transforms). Example backfill trace:
```
worker=backfill-7 handling prefix=rw-prod-abc123 origin_latency=4.2s status=200
worker=backfill-2 handling prefix=rw-prod-xyz789 origin_latency=7.9s status=504
```
Leo Patel: That 504s explain the customer errors. Origin burst is saturating transform CPUs. We should slow invalidator permanently until repopulate baseline is restored.
Emily S.: Immediate mitigation plan: 1) keep invalidator paused (done), 2) enqueue prioritized warm requests for top N prefixes (top 200 by qps), 3) temporarily raise backfill workers from 10 -> 30, 4) enable graceful degrade: serve stale entries for 30s. Any objections?
Julian M.: No objections. I'm ready to run the prioritized warm script. Script: `python tools/prioritize_warm.py --top 200 --dryrun` then remove --dryrun to execute.
Ava Chen: Running warmers (removing dryrun). Will monitor queue.
SRE-Ryan: I'll scale backfill deployment: `kubectl scale deployment/backfill --replicas=30 -n cache` — doing now.
Leo Patel: Watching origin latency and error rate. Also starting a quick timeline note: 13:58 config push -> 14:00 invalidation full-scan triggered -> 14:01-14:06 mass deletes -> 14:05 alert fired -> 14:07 invalidator paused -> 14:09 backfill scaled, warmers started -> 14:22 partial hit recovery observed on subset prefixes.
Ava Chen: Observed hitrate bump to ~48% on warm-prioritized prefixes at 14:26. Overall cluster still 62% miss. Customer impact: tickets still coming but new errors decreasing.
Emily S.: For the RCA we need to capture: exact invalidator rule change, who approved config push, whether scanning logic had a new wildcard behavior, and whether TTLs were unintentionally shortened. Also collect sample keys that were deleted.
Julian M.: I pulled invalidator config diff: `git diff infra/invalidator/configs/prod.yaml@HEAD~1..HEAD` shows `pattern: rw-prod-*` changed to `pattern: rw-prod-*` (sorry) — actual change was `mode: delta` -> `mode: aggressive` in last commit (author: infra-ops-bot). That matches aggressive full purge behavior.
Ava Chen: Why was aggressive mode toggled? @infra-ops-bot or ops team?
Emily S.: notification from config-approvals: PR #2145 merged by infra-ops-bot as auto-merge — owner listed as infra/cron. I'll tag the PR in the RCA.
SRE-Ryan: Next steps for incident: 1) capture full timeline and log bundle (logs.redwood link saved), 2) collect list of impacted prefixes and customers, 3) postmortem owners: infra for config change, cache team for backfill capacity, product for support comms.
Leo Patel: Adding quick mitigation follow-ups: - add rate-limit on invalidator deletes to 100 prefixes/sec, - add kill-switch endpoint with RBAC, - add preflight check for mode changes in CI.
Julian M.: I'll create a timeline doc and upload logs: https://internal.redwood/docs/incident/1977012345/timeline (uploading now).
Ava Chen: Customer comms draft: "We experienced a transient regression in cache performance due to a configuration change to the invalidation job; we've paused the job, repopulated hot prefixes, and are rolling a config rollback. Some customers saw higher latency/errors between 14:00-14:10 UTC. Root cause and full RCA forthcoming."
Emily S.: I'll loop in support and comms and share the draft.
SRE-Ryan: For now keep invalidator paused until we validate safe mode. Also add a post-incident runbook: revert mode->delta, implement gradual delete window, and add auto-detect for mass-delete anomalies.
Ava Chen: Confirmed rollback PR opened (#2148) reverting mode. Rolling out to canary first, then prod after 30m validation.
Leo Patel: Monitoring: hitrate back to 81% at 14:45, errors nearly cleared. Closing live mitigation channel when rollbacks validated.
incident-bot: Incident acknowledged as mitigated; create postmortem and link under incidents/1977012345-postmortem when ready. :white_check_mark:
Ava Chen: I'll own the postmortem writeup, will include timeline, root cause (aggressive invalidator mode + no rate-limit), impact numbers, and follow-up actions.
Julian M.: Adding action items to the doc: (1) CI preflight for invalidator mode (owner: infra), (2) delete-rate limit (owner: cache), (3) backfill autoscale policy (owner: cache), (4) support comms template (owner: product).
Emily S.: Thx everyone. Leaving notes for SRE followup on-call.
SRE-Ryan: standing by until rollback is validated. Good job team. :thumbsup:
text:
incident-bot: INCIDENT DETECTED — prefix cache miss rate jumped 7x for prod-cluster-usw2 at 14:05 UTC; threshold breached. Paging oncall. :rotating_light:
Ava Chen: I'm on it, got the page. Looking at dashboards now (console -> /dash/cache/hitrate).
Leo Patel: seeing huge tail of misses on model-prefix 'rw-prod-*' across nodes, hitrate down from 92% -> 18% in 6m. Linking logs: https://logs.redwood.example/prod/stream?query=prefix_miss
Emily S.: any recent deploys?
Ava Chen: Nope, no code deploy in last 3 hours. There was a config push to cache-invalidator at 13:58 (infra-cfg-2026-03-15) that touched prefix TTLs.
Julian M.: I can check invalidation job timeline. Looks like the invalidator kicked a full-scan at 14:01 (job-id: inv-7801).
SRE-Ryan: paging handoff in 10m — adding myself as secondary. Quick mitigation: can we pause invalidator?
Ava Chen: Trying to find pause endpoint. inv-service has /admin/scan/pause but needs admin token. Running: `kubectl exec -n infra deploy/inv-service -- curl -sS -X POST http://localhost:8080/admin/scan/pause`
Leo Patel: Logs from inv-service show a flood of delete events for prefixes matching `rw-prod-*` starting 14:00:05. snippet:
```
2026-03-15T14:00:05Z INFO invalidator deleting prefix rw-prod-abc123
2026-03-15T14:00:06Z INFO invalidator deleting prefix rw-prod-xyz789
... (thousands)
```
Ava Chen: Executed pause, response 200. Confirmed job stopped; however hitrate still low — many requests now missing and being recomputed from origin, causing latency and customer errors.
Emily S.: Customer impact check: error budget alert shows 5s P95 latency spiked; error tracker flagged 6 customers with degraded responses (IDs: cust-821, cust-914...). Support already got inbound tickets.
Julian M.: hypothesis 1: invalidator purge was too aggressive due to wildcard misconfig (bad regex), evicted warm prefixes. hypothesis 2: TTL mis-sync across cache tiers causing faster expiry + invalidator scan coincident.
SRE-Ryan: Important: are we seeing cache-populate/backfill traffic? If backfilling is slow it explains sustained miss rate.
Ava Chen: Backfill logs show worker pool is overwhelmed; workers stuck on heavy origin requests (long tail transforms). Example backfill trace:
```
worker=backfill-7 handling prefix=rw-prod-abc123 origin_latency=4.2s status=200
worker=backfill-2 handling prefix=rw-prod-xyz789 origin_latency=7.9s status=504
```
Leo Patel: That 504s explain the customer errors. Origin burst is saturating transform CPUs. We should slow invalidator permanently until repopulate baseline is restored.
Emily S.: Immediate mitigation plan: 1) keep invalidator paused (done), 2) enqueue prioritized warm requests for top N prefixes (top 200 by qps), 3) temporarily raise backfill workers from 10 -> 30, 4) enable graceful degrade: serve stale entries for 30s. Any objections?
Julian M.: No objections. I'm ready to run the prioritized warm script. Script: `python tools/prioritize_warm.py --top 200 --dryrun` then remove --dryrun to execute.
Ava Chen: Running warmers (removing dryrun). Will monitor queue.
SRE-Ryan: I'll scale backfill deployment: `kubectl scale deployment/backfill --replicas=30 -n cache` — doing now.
Leo Patel: Watching origin latency and error rate. Also starting a quick timeline note: 13:58 config push -> 14:00 invalidation full-scan triggered -> 14:01-14:06 mass deletes -> 14:05 alert fired -> 14:07 invalidator paused -> 14:09 backfill scaled, warmers started -> 14:22 partial hit recovery observed on subset prefixes.
Ava Chen: Observed hitrate bump to ~48% on warm-prioritized prefixes at 14:26. Overall cluster still 62% miss. Customer impact: tickets still coming but new errors decreasing.
Emily S.: For the RCA we need to capture: exact invalidator rule change, who approved config push, whether scanning logic had a new wildcard behavior, and whether TTLs were unintentionally shortened. Also collect sample keys that were deleted.
Julian M.: I pulled invalidator config diff: `git diff infra/invalidator/configs/prod.yaml@HEAD~1..HEAD` shows `pattern: rw-prod-*` changed to `pattern: rw-prod-*` (sorry) — actual change was `mode: delta` -> `mode: aggressive` in last commit (author: infra-ops-bot). That matches aggressive full purge behavior.
Ava Chen: Why was aggressive mode toggled? @infra-ops-bot or ops team?
Emily S.: notification from config-approvals: PR #2145 merged by infra-ops-bot as auto-merge — owner listed as infra/cron. I'll tag the PR in the RCA.
SRE-Ryan: Next steps for incident: 1) capture full timeline and log bundle (logs.redwood link saved), 2) collect list of impacted prefixes and customers, 3) postmortem owners: infra for config change, cache team for backfill capacity, product for support comms.
Leo Patel: Adding quick mitigation follow-ups: - add rate-limit on invalidator deletes to 100 prefixes/sec, - add kill-switch endpoint with RBAC, - add preflight check for mode changes in CI.
Julian M.: I'll create a timeline doc and upload logs: https://internal.redwood/docs/incident/1977012345/timeline (uploading now).
Ava Chen: Customer comms draft: "We experienced a transient regression in cache performance due to a configuration change to the invalidation job; we've paused the job, repopulated hot prefixes, and are rolling a config rollback. Some customers saw higher latency/errors between 14:00-14:10 UTC. Root cause and full RCA forthcoming."
Emily S.: I'll loop in support and comms and share the draft.
SRE-Ryan: For now keep invalidator paused until we validate safe mode. Also add a post-incident runbook: revert mode->delta, implement gradual delete window, and add auto-detect for mass-delete anomalies.
Ava Chen: Confirmed rollback PR opened (#2148) reverting mode. Rolling out to canary first, then prod after 30m validation.
Leo Patel: Monitoring: hitrate back to 81% at 14:45, errors nearly cleared. Closing live mitigation channel when rollbacks validated.
incident-bot: Incident acknowledged as mitigated; create postmortem and link under incidents/1977012345-postmortem when ready. :white_check_mark:
Ava Chen: I'll own the postmortem writeup, will include timeline, root cause (aggressive invalidator mode + no rate-limit), impact numbers, and follow-up actions.
Julian M.: Adding action items to the doc: (1) CI preflight for invalidator mode (owner: infra), (2) delete-rate limit (owner: cache), (3) backfill autoscale policy (owner: cache), (4) support comms template (owner: product).
Emily S.: Thx everyone. Leaving notes for SRE followup on-call.
SRE-Ryan: standing by until rollback is validated. Good job team. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1972500000
preview:
Elena Voss: quick follow-up from the postmortem  we listed remediation items but didn't lock owners for runbook/playbook edits and recurrence tracking. can folks volunteer?
Harper Lee: I can own the triage steps + safe-check snippets. target: Fri EOD.
Mateo Alvarez: I'll take the escalation matrix and oncall paging thresholds. aim for next Wed.
Jin Park: I can add recurrence tagging automation & a saved query, plus a short playbook section. target: Tue.
Elena Voss: great. please paste file paths and commit dates so I can update the action tracker. also include a tiny eval in PRs (MTTI before vs after).
Harper Lee: triage runbook will include idempotent commands and a verification checklist. sample:
```
# get p95 for model
rwinfctl metrics query --model=chat-main --window=5m --stat=p95
# dry-run route switch
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100 --dry-run
# apply when confirmed (idempotent)
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100
```
Mateo Alvarez: paging change proposal: extend transient p95 window from 5m -> 10m for alerts, add step: "confirm spike not from scheduled batch job" before paging. I'll annotate staging vs prod.
Jin Park: recurrence tagging: label recurrence=yes and field recurrence_count. saved filter: "recurrence:yes AND created>=-30d". will add grafana tile for "recurring incidents" and link in playbook.
ops-bot: created TKT-9988 (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/TKT-9988) "Runbook & Playbook edits from 1972499990 outage" assigned to Harper, Mateo, Jin. Due dates set per thread.

title: #incidents: 1955009999
preview:
Priya Shah: Heads-up: multiple EU customers reporting spikes in latency and auth failures since ~09:10 UTC, forwarded from support. They say requests are routing to APAC nodes (200+ ms to 5000+ ms).
Dan Li: I'm seeing an automated change from routing-map service at 09:08 UTC — a label rename for `eu-west-v2` -> `eu-west-2` that shouldn't have happened. Could that have broken region matching?
Mateo Alvarez: Logs show fallback policy kicked in due to a route mismatch. Sample trace: `req=abc123 region=eu-west expected_host=eu-west-1 redir=ap-sg-1 latency=2.1s auth_status=401`
Priya Shah: Customers also mention intermittent 401s and duplicate billing events for a few requests (CS raised billing alarms). Not all customers affected, seems scoped to certain prefixes.
OpsBot: ALERT: routing-map-service anomaly detected: 09:07:44 config-update user=infra-bot change=label-rename routing-map/v1
Elena R.: Confirmed — the label rename triggered the fallback rule that prefers any matching region name; when `eu-west-v2` didn't match, fallback matched `*-west` pattern and selected APAC pool due to ordering bug.
Dan Li: Quick repro for devs: `curl -H "Host: api.redwood.com" -H "X-Region: eu-west-v2" https://api.redwood.com/v1/generate` — observe `x-served-region: ap-sg-1` and `x-token-check: kms-region-mismatch`.
Mateo Alvarez: Repro logs attached in the ticket (pasted):
```
REQUEST id=abc123 headers={X-Region: eu-west-v2}
ROUTER match=label_name==eu-west-v2 -> no match
ROUTER fallback=pattern=*west -> matched ap-sg-1
INFER runtime=ap-sg-1 auth=kms-eu -> auth fail KMS region mismatch
```
Sara Kim: Support: we've told impacted customers we're investigating and provided temporary workaround (set `X-Region` to `eu-west-1` if possible). A few enterprise customers want a timeline.
Priya Shah: Immediate mitigation performed at 09:25 UTC: rolled back label rename and applied a strict-match hotfix to routing policy. Traffic normalization starting ~09:28 UTC.
Dan Li: Mitigations we applied: 1) rollback config 2) hotpatch to require exact label equality before pattern fallback 3) temporarily disable automated label-sync from infra-bot.
Mateo Alvarez: Confirmed post-rollback traces show requests served by eu-west pools again and auth handshakes succeeding. Sample: `req=def456 x-served-region=eu-west-1 auth_status=200 latency=120ms`
Priya Shah: Support update: majority of affected customers report restored service. Two enterprise accounts still saw delayed token charges; billing team is investigating root cause of duplicates.
Elena R.: Root cause summary: an automated label-sync job renamed `eu-west-v2` to `eu-west-2` (typo) and our router's fallback matcher had a precedence bug that allowed broad pattern matches to prefer APAC in absence of exact label. This created cross-region routing and KMS region mismatches -> 401s and elevated latency.
Dan Li: Why did label-sync rename happen? It was a downstream normalization script for region aliases. The script applies transforms without validating against the active routing map.
Sara Kim: CS note: customers want assurance this won't recur. We need a customer-friendly postmortem and a list of compensations for billing anomalies.
Priya Shah: Action items (owners):
- Dan: add validation to label-sync to check active routing map before renames + test (ETA 48h)
- Elena: add unit + e2e test for router precedence (ETA 72h)
- Mateo: create alert for KMS region mismatch spikes (ETA 24h)
- Finance/Sara: reconcile billing duplicates and propose credits (ETA 72h)
- Ops: reintroduce automated label-sync behind canary and gating (ETA 1 week)
Dan Li: Temporary safeguards: revert automation to manual approval; I pushed a gate requiring human confirm for region label changes.
Mateo Alvarez: Postmortem draft suggestions: include timeline, root cause (label-sync typo + router precedence bug), impact (latency, 401s, billing dupes), mitigations, long-term fixes, and customer communication.
Priya Shah: I'll draft initial customer-facing note and the internal postmortem. Draft ETA 4 hours, circulating for review.
SupportBot: Ticket update created: INC-2026 linked to this thread. Impacted customers: list attached.
Elena R.: Also recommend: add a canary that verifies KMS handshake region before accepting routing-map changes.
Sara Kim: I'll prepare SLAs and proposed compensations; want approval from finance before sending.
Dan Li: After hotfix, deploy pipeline will include a static analysis check that flags renames which change canonical region tokens.
Priya Shah: Thanks team. I'll post postmortem link in this thread when ready and coordinate customer comms with Sara/Finance.
Elena R.: Temporary retrospective note — we had a single point where human-readable label normalization could alter programmatic identifiers. We'll treat labels as immutable identifiers going forward.
Priya Shah: Closing updates: service restored fully by 09:30 UTC, monitoring for 48h, follow-ups scheduled.
Dan Li: I'll add the issue to the infra backlog and tag for high priority.
Mateo Alvarez: Adding remedial test to CI now.
Priya Shah: Great work on rapid rollback. We'll sync tomorrow 10:00 UTC for a quick runthrough before the postmortem goes public. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1934567890
preview:
Alex Kim: FYI SFO GPUs saw a sustained eviction spike at 02:12 UTC, dropping available capacity ~18% for ~14m. Triggered weighted shift but some clients hit tail latency spikes.

Maya Patel: Thanks. Do we know root cause? evictions from host churn or node OOMs?

Alex Kim: Early signs point to simultaneous node reboots after a kernel patch + aggressive kube eviction threshold (we changed default from 0.5 -> 0.7 last week). Node churn + large batch jobs failed to drain cleanly.

Juan Ortega: We saw burst autoscale try to allocate, but reserve pool ran dry. Request routing fell back to cross-region which added 30-40ms p99.

Sophie Li: Key ask: update runbook to smooth eviction reaction and add cooldown before shifting full weight. Current playbook immediate 100% shift is too aggressive.

Ben Carter: Proposed changes:
1) Add 10m cooldown after first eviction surge before 100% shift.
2) Progressive weights: 25% -> 50% -> 75% -> 100% over 5m steps if eviction persists.
3) Increase reserve pool target from 10% -> 15% for SFO during peak hours.
4) Add automatic billing tag when burst capacity > 30% to flag cost impact.

Priya Rao: Also add observability hooks: per-node eviction rate metric, per-pod preemption counter, and a dashboard panel with "reserve_remaining" + "burst_cost_estimate".

Alex Kim: I'll PR playbook edits. Draft snippet for traffic shift helper:
```
# progressive shift helper (pseudo)
for w in [25,50,75,100]:
  curl -XPOST https://routing.redwood/api/shift -d '{"region":"sfo","weight":'+str(w)+'}'
  sleep 60
```

ops-bot: Incident created: INC-2026-1934567890, severity=high, timeline attached.

Maya Patel: Action items:
- Alex -> update runbook + add cooldown logic (due EOD UTC)
- Priya -> dashboard panels + alert (due +48h)
- Ben -> reserve pool sizing proposal + cost estimate (due +72h)
- Juan -> add unit tests for progressive shift helper (due +72h)

Sophie Li: Add an explicit rollback step to return weights back to baseline if latency improves; add a canary check to validate p99 under 150ms for 5m before increasing to next weight.

Ben Carter: Noted. Will include canary gating in proposal.

Alex Kim: Quick followups: need a short KB blurb so support can explain the burst cost tag to customers. I'll draft.

Juan Ortega: Temporary mitigation: we've enabled a 12m warm pool pre-provision during peak window for SFO, lowered batch job concurrency where possible.

Priya Rao: I added the alert template: "SFO eviction_rate > 0.2 for 3m" -> runbook link. Will push to staging.

Sophie Li: Thanks all. Let's reconvene after PRs land. :thumbsup: 

Ben Carter: ack. (reaction :white_check_mark:)

incident-bot: Incident INC-2026-1934567890 status updated -> monitoring.

title: #incidents: 1912507890
preview:
Sam Ortiz: quick postmortem follow-up — we captured the remediation list but didn't assign owners for runbook/playbook edits or recurrence tracking. can we split ownership?
Nora Blake: I can take the triage playbook (low-latency path). target: next Mon EOD.
Ethan Park: I'll own the escalation flow and oncall paging matrix. aiming for Wed.
Ravi Desai: I will add recurrence tagging automation in the incident tracker and a short playbook section for recurring patterns. Tue for initial draft.
Sam Ortiz: awesome. can we also decide what commands go into the runbook (safe checks + idempotent ops)?
Nora Blake: yes. i'll add safe-checks and an explicit rollback snippet. will keep commands copy-paste ready.
Nora Blake: ```
# check p95 before acting
rwinfctl metrics get --model=chat-main --window=5m | jq '.p95'
# if sustained above threshold for 3 samples, dry-run route change
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100 --dry-run
# confirm and apply (idempotent)
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100

title: #incidents: 1905007890
preview:
oncall-bot: INCIDENT alert 1905007890 — service degradation: text-gen endpoints (acmepay-prod) reporting high latency and intermittent 504s. paging @Samir (oncall).
oncall-bot: runbook link: https://redwood-internal/runbooks/kv-cache-eviction  — incident channel created.

Samir Patel: I'm on it. seeing 95th latency ~18s, error rate spike to 2.3%. initial hypothesis: KV cache thrash on the dedicated pool, GC spikes + model fallback causing long tail. scaling up pool now.

Liz Chen: pinged support and product ops, we have 3 customer tickets from AcmePay. they're seeing slow invoice generation. CC @Maria.

Maria Gomez: thanks. prioritizing outreach to affected accounts. Samir, any ETA for mitigation?

Samir Patel: scaled pool + forced warm replicas; started rolling restart for nodes showing high cache misses. early telemetry: errors down to 0.8%, p95 moving toward 7000ms. still higher than baseline.

Jordan Blake: I'm tracking logs, will run the request-replay against a canary cluster to reproduce. leaving notes for handoff at 07:00 UTC if needed.

Samir Patel: quick log excerpt: ```
[03:10:12] WARN kvcache: eviction count=1432, miss_rate=0.78
[03:11:03] ERROR serve/runtime: model-fallback triggered for route=/v1/generate, reason=kvcache-miss
[03:12:01] WARN infra: pod node-gpu-4 oom_kill_count=1
```

Liz Chen: good call to capture OOM/kills. does this map to any recent deploy? Release bot — any deploys to acmepay pool in the last 24h?

devops-bot: No production deploys to acmepay-prod in last 24h. Autoscaler bumped pool sizes at 02:58 UTC due to traffic surge.

Samir Patel: looks like traffic spike + previously noisy long-running requests caused the cache budget to be exhausted and evictions to cascade. fallback to smaller model increased per-request CPU, further exacerbating OOMs.

Maria Gomez: customer impact summary for postmortem: ~10% of AcmePay requests affected for ~22 minutes, some hit 504s. Support reported 3 high-priority cases, no chargebacks reported yet.

Liz Chen: scheduling postmortem: propose tomorrow 16:00 PT / 23:00 UTC. agenda: timeline, contributing factors, action items, customer communications. who can attend?

Samir Patel: I'll prepare the initial RCA timeline and upload a draft.

Jordan Blake: I'll own repro and the cache tuning experiment.

Alex Rivera: I'll own automated alert thresholds and will create a quick dashboard to monitor kvcache eviction rate by pool.

Maria Gomez: I'll handle customer comms and sign-off on impact language. please make sure support ticket links are in the doc.

Samir Patel: uploaded RCA draft: https://redwood-internal/rca/1905007890-draft — request reviews from @Liz, @Jordan, @Alex.

Liz Chen: reading now. quick comment: timeline misses the autoscaler bump at 02:58 — pls add. Also suggest an explicit rollback/fallback policy so we don't trigger heavier models automatically when cache pressure is high.

Samir Patel: noted and added. also added mitigation snippet to run as emergency runbook: ```
# emergency mitigation
kubectl scale deployment acme-gen --replicas=12 -n acmepay-prod
kubectl rollout restart deployment acme-gen -n acmepay-prod --field-selector=status.phase=Running
```

Jordan Blake: left inline comments about load pattern — attaching reproducer script: https://redwood-internal/tools/reproducers/acmepay-spike.sh. needs review for safety on prod.

Liz Chen: peer review checklist: 1) clear timeline, 2) impact numbers, 3) contributing factors, 4) action owners and due dates, 5) customer comms & postmortem note. once checklist done, we'll sign off.

Alex Rivera: created Jira RED-3421 to track remediation actions (link: https://jira.redwood/RED-3421). I've added sub-tasks: alert tuning, cache-reservation experiment, dashboard, postmortem meeting notes.

Samir Patel: proposed action items (with owners):
- Add eviction-rate alert (Alex) — due EOD +3
- Adjust autoscaler policy to prefer burst capac to avoid model fallback (Jordan) — due EOD +7
- Implement cache reservation for critical tenants (engineering) — owner Samir — due EOD +14
- Postmortem writeup + customer note (Maria/Liz) — due EOD +2

Maria Gomez: will draft customer-facing blurb after RCA final. pls keep me looped.

Liz Chen: small process ask — include a one-paragraph TL;DR at top of RCA for execs.

Samir Patel: updated RCA with TL;DR and action table. requesting peer review signoffs. @Liz, @Jordan, @Alex — please add :white_check_mark: when you approve.

Jordan Blake: reviewed, added a couple of clarifying comments, approving now. ✅

Alex Rivera: approved. :white_check_mark:

Liz Chen: one last wording tweak on customer impact section, approved. ✅

Samir Patel: thanks everyone — document marked "peer-reviewed" and moved to final draft. requesting final sign-off from @Maria for customer comms and from compliance if needed.

Maria Gomez: signed off on customer language, sending to AcmePay contacts now. :email:

devops-bot: Action items for RED-3421 updated: eviction alert assigned to Alex Rivera, due date set.

Samir Patel: closing loop — mitigation verified, no further errors for last 3 hours. I'll follow up in postmortem meeting with more telemetry and root cause writeup.

oncall-bot: Incident 1905007890 resolved and transitioned to post-incident tracking. If there are any follow-ups outside RED-3421, please file them in the retro thread. :lock:

title: #incidents: 1852009876
preview:
PagerDuty: Incident triggered: MET-INGEST-ANOMALY (severity: P1)
Service: metering-ingest
Alert: token_count delta > 20% vs baseline for 15m
Link: https://pagerduty.example/INC12345

incident-bot: page fired to oncall (priaya_oncall). Runbook suggested: metering ingestion restart + backfill.

Priya Rao: ack. I'm looking — seeing spike in duplicate token counts from kafka-partition-4. Pulling logs and consumer offsets.

Mateo Alvarez: I can help with consumer side. Any chance aggregator job is stuck? Last commit offset seen 1245789.

Priya Rao: tail of aggregator logs:
```
2026-03-14T02:45:22Z ERROR aggregator failed to flush batch: timeout
2026-03-14T02:45:22Z WARN duplicate token detected count=3421 partition=4
2026-03-14T02:45:23Z INFO retrying batch flush
```

Jun Park: escalating to SRE lead. Priya, have you tried a rolling restart of the aggregator pods `metering-agg-prod`? Could be KV cache growth or stuck IO.

Priya Rao: planning restart now. Doing `kubectl rollout restart deployment/metering-agg-prod` (rolling). Will monitor lag.

BillingBot: ALERT: 43 customer invoices currently queued waiting on token reconciliation. If pipeline stays stalled invoices may be delayed.

Sophie (CS): flagged top customers and opened support threads. Prep notification template for possible invoice delay.

Mateo Alvarez: restart triggered, pods cycling. Also temporarily scaling replicas to 6 to help drain backlog.

Priya Rao: pods back up. Consumer lag dropped from ~150k -> ~20k offsets. Token delta reduced to ~3%. Continuing to monitor for 10m.

incident-bot: PagerDuty: incident acknowledged by Priya Rao (priaya_oncall). Escalation paused.

Jun Park: good mitigation. Next step: re-commit stuck offsets or run backfill consumer. I'll prep the re-commit job in 10m in case backlog doesn't finish.

Priya Rao: starting re-commit job `metering-recommit-2026-03-14` now with `--max-batch=2000` to avoid big spikes.

BillingBot: Update: re-commit started. Estimated throughput ~2k tokens/sec; estimated catch-up time ~2.5 hours.

Sophie (CS): updating customers with ETA ~3h, message drafted: "We are processing a backlog which may delay invoices by up to 3 hours — engineering is actively remediating." :phone:

Priya Rao: handing off to night oncall (Jun Park) at 04:00 UTC. Leaving runbook notes and dashboards in the thread.

Jun Park: ack handoff. I'll watch the re-commit and escalate to Eng Infra if throughput drops. Good job team :thumbsup:

PagerDuty: Status -> monitoring. If metrics (token delta, consumer lag) stay within thresholds for 30m we'll resolve the incident.

-- end thread

title: #incidents: 1845129999
preview:
Samir Patel: Postmortem TL;DR: repeated partial DB pool saturations over last 3 months. The postmortem lists fixes, but we need a short-term playbook change + medium-term automation to stop the recurrence. Who can own?

Lina Ortiz: I'll take the playbook edits for triage steps. We should add a 'pattern-detection' section so oncall can flag if the same metric/profile has fired in the last 30 days.

Ethan Park: Agree. Also can we add explicit 'what-not-to-do' notes (e.g., don't restart pgbouncer repeatedly during ramp). That was a contributor in one event.

Riley Ford: For automation, propose a weekly job that scans alerts and flags duplicates with >=2 occurrences in 30d and auto-creates a ticket with links to postmortems. I can draft the script.

Samir Patel: Nice. Lina, add these items to the runbook: (1) canonical triage commands (2) pattern check step (3) immediate mitigations (eg traffic-shed to read replicas) (4) explicit anti-patterns. Please include command examples.

Lina Ortiz: Will do. Command examples to include:
```
# check pgbouncer status
systemctl status pgbouncer || journalctl -u pgbouncer -n 200
# active vs waiting clients
psql -h primary -c "select sum(case when state='active' then 1 else 0 end) as active, sum(case when state='waiting' then 1 else 0 end) as waiting from pgbouncer_stats;"
# connection failures rate (from prometheus)
curl -s "http://prometheus/api/v1/query?query=rate(pgbouncer_connection_failures_total[5m])"
```

Ethan Park: For the escalation decision tree: if waiting_clients > 800 for 5m OR failure_rate > 30% for 3m -> run mitigation workflow and page DB oncall. If recurring pattern detected (>=2 similar incidents in 30d) -> page infra lead immediately.

PagerBot: Alert summary: 3 similar incidents in last 90 days. Auto-created ticket: INF-9876.

Riley Ford: I'll prototype the duplicate-alert scanner and open a PR. Timeline: draft by 2026-04-08. It will create a label 'recurring-incident' and auto-link postmortems.

Samir Patel: Owners & due dates:
- Runbook playbook edits (owner: Lina)  due: 2026-04-04
- Anti-pattern notes & triage commands (owner: Ethan)  due: 2026-04-03
- Duplicate-alert scanner (owner: Riley)  due: 2026-04-08
- Weekly incident review meeting to triage recurring patterns (owner: Samir)  start: 2026-04-06 recurring weekly

Lina Ortiz: I'll add a short 'how to run pattern check' snippet in the runbook and include links to INF-9876 and the postmortem. Creating GH issues now.

Ethan Park: I'll draft the "do not" checklist and include a safe restart flow (circuit-breaker + confirmation step). EOD tomorrow.

Samir Patel: Thanks all. We'll track progress in the GH issues and sync next Tuesday. If anyone hits blockers on access/permissions for automation, ping me privately.

title: #incidents: 1845012345
preview:
Priya Nair: Quick follow-up from last night's pgbouncer incident — postmortem is up but we still need clear owners and due dates for runbook/playbook edits. Proposing a 2-week sprint to close the top-4 action items. Thoughts?

Ben Liu: I can own runbook edits for the connection-storm scenario. I'll split it into (1) triage checklist (2) escalation & decision tree (3) automation suggestions.

Ava Chen: Can we standardize the exact commands to run? Last night people ran slightly different checks which slowed triage. Suggest adding a minimal set to the runbook. Example snippet below we can copy:

```
sudo systemctl status pgbouncer
psql -h primary -c "select count(*) from pg_stat_activity;"
pgbouncer-admin show clients
# if many WAITING, run: pgbouncer-admin show pools
```

Josh Miller: I'll add the 'when-to-failover' decision tree and update on-call contacts. @Ben @Priya please confirm thresholds for when we escalate to DB oncall.

OpsBot: INCIDENT #2026-142 closed — root cause: client connection leak causing pool exhaustion. Postmortem uploaded: https://redwood.internal/postmortems/2026-142

Priya Nair: Action items (short):
- Runbook: canonical triage commands + who runs them (owner: Ben) — due 2026-04-05
- Escalation path: updated paging & decision tree (owner: Josh) — due 2026-04-07
- Auto-alert tuning & circuit-breaker proposal (owner: Maya) — due 2026-04-09
- Postmortem tabletop & smoke test (owner: Ava) — due 2026-04-12
I'll create GH issues and label them postmortem-2026-142.

Maya Gomez: I'll take auto-alert tuning. Proposal: add a short 'circuit breaker' that trips when connection-failure-rate > 20% for 2m and active_client_rate spike to > p95 baseline. Route traffic to replicas during the TTL. Also add metric: `pgbouncer.active_client_rate` 5m p95.

Ben Liu: :thumbsup: I'll produce a minimal triage checklist with exact commands and a fast-fail step to restart pgbouncer only after X retries. Draft by EOD tomorrow and will link the GH issue.

Ava Chen: Tabletop schedule: propose 2026-04-10 15:00 UTC — 1 hour. Agenda: reproduce load pattern, simulate connection leak, practice failover steps, verify alert triggers. I'll create the calendar invite and include DB, infra, and product SMEs.

Josh Miller: Updated escalation summary for runbook: page DB oncall when either (a) >1000 waiting clients for 5m OR (b) connection failure rate >50% for 3m. If DB oncall unavailable, page SRE lead. e2e snippet for runbook: 

```escalation
1) Alert fires
2) Run triage commands (see triage checklist)
3) If threshold exceeded, page DB oncall (call + text)
4) If no ack in 5m, page SRE lead and initiate read-replica routing
```

Priya Nair: :white_check_mark: Perfect. I'll open the GH issues, assign owners, and schedule weekly check-ins in #incidents to track progress. If anyone needs extra bandwidth for implementation, ping me. Thanks everyone :eyes:
text:
Priya Nair: Quick follow-up from last night's pgbouncer incident — postmortem is up but we still need clear owners and due dates for runbook/playbook edits. Proposing a 2-week sprint to close the top-4 action items. Thoughts?

Ben Liu: I can own runbook edits for the connection-storm scenario. I'll split it into (1) triage checklist (2) escalation & decision tree (3) automation suggestions.

Ava Chen: Can we standardize the exact commands to run? Last night people ran slightly different checks which slowed triage. Suggest adding a minimal set to the runbook. Example snippet below we can copy:

```
sudo systemctl status pgbouncer
psql -h primary -c "select count(*) from pg_stat_activity;"
pgbouncer-admin show clients
# if many WAITING, run: pgbouncer-admin show pools
```

Josh Miller: I'll add the 'when-to-failover' decision tree and update on-call contacts. @Ben @Priya please confirm thresholds for when we escalate to DB oncall.

OpsBot: INCIDENT #2026-142 closed — root cause: client connection leak causing pool exhaustion. Postmortem uploaded: https://redwood.internal/postmortems/2026-142

Priya Nair: Action items (short):
- Runbook: canonical triage commands + who runs them (owner: Ben) — due 2026-04-05
- Escalation path: updated paging & decision tree (owner: Josh) — due 2026-04-07
- Auto-alert tuning & circuit-breaker proposal (owner: Maya) — due 2026-04-09
- Postmortem tabletop & smoke test (owner: Ava) — due 2026-04-12
I'll create GH issues and label them postmortem-2026-142.

Maya Gomez: I'll take auto-alert tuning. Proposal: add a short 'circuit breaker' that trips when connection-failure-rate > 20% for 2m and active_client_rate spike to > p95 baseline. Route traffic to replicas during the TTL. Also add metric: `pgbouncer.active_client_rate` 5m p95.

Ben Liu: :thumbsup: I'll produce a minimal triage checklist with exact commands and a fast-fail step to restart pgbouncer only after X retries. Draft by EOD tomorrow and will link the GH issue.

Ava Chen: Tabletop schedule: propose 2026-04-10 15:00 UTC — 1 hour. Agenda: reproduce load pattern, simulate connection leak, practice failover steps, verify alert triggers. I'll create the calendar invite and include DB, infra, and product SMEs.

Josh Miller: Updated escalation summary for runbook: page DB oncall when either (a) >1000 waiting clients for 5m OR (b) connection failure rate >50% for 3m. If DB oncall unavailable, page SRE lead. e2e snippet for runbook: 

```escalation
1) Alert fires
2) Run triage commands (see triage checklist)
3) If threshold exceeded, page DB oncall (call + text)
4) If no ack in 5m, page SRE lead and initiate read-replica routing
```

Priya Nair: :white_check_mark: Perfect. I'll open the GH issues, assign owners, and schedule weekly check-ins in #incidents to track progress. If anyone needs extra bandwidth for implementation, ping me. Thanks everyone :eyes:

title: #incidents: 1829991111
preview:
incidents-bot: INCIDENT ALERT: elevated 5xx rate observed in control-plane traffic. Triggered alarm: controlplane.gateway.5xx_rate > 10% for 3m. Affected subsystems flagged: auth-service, org-lookup, config-service. Pager escalated. See https://grafana.redwood.example/d/controlplane/alerts (root ts: 1829991111)
Samir Patel: on it. seeing a big spike starting ~1829991080. top error codes 502/504 and timeouts. Service map shows increased latency between gateway -> auth (p95 2.4s) and org-lookup -> cassandra timeouts. Can someone pull recent deploys/changes?
Juniper (infra): checking deploy history... nothing high volume for auth in last hour, but gateway had a config push ~1829991050 that adjusted header validation and request batching. sending logs.
Ava Chen: I can reproduce via curl: returned HTTP 502 with body: "upstream error: timeout waiting for response". Trace id 0a3f-7b2c. Example: ```curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <redacted>" -v https://api.redwood.example/v1/models``` response headers show `x-upstream-timeout: 1200ms`.
Marta Ruiz: paging SRE oncall to triage. Impact: hosted API customers are getting errors on generation and embeddings endpoints intermittently. Approx 15% of requests failing for a subset of routes. Need prioritized mitigation: 1) circuit-break auth calls for non-critical endpoints, 2) enable read-only cache for org metadata, 3) consider rolling back gateway config.
pagerduty-bot: Page sent to SRE (oncall). Escalation policy: control-plane-high. Pagerduty incident: PD-98765.
Samir Patel: quick mitigation plan: throttle bulk/ingest routes at the gateway (temporarily block /v1/ingest/*), enable cached-lookups for org-lookup path, and reduce auth validation strictness (fail open where safe). Who can flip gateway flags?
Liam O'Neill: I can flip flags. Preparing patch to gate off the new header validation and reduce batch size. Will push change to staging then prod. ETA ~6 mins.
Juniper (infra): logs show many `java.net.SocketTimeoutException` from org-lookup when talking to cassandra cluster in us-east-1b. Also connection pool exhaustion on org-lookup due to sudden backpressure from gateway batching. Could be the new batching config increasing concurrent downstream requests.
Ava Chen: running a targeted test to validate rollback risk. Also capturing a heap of traces for the last 10m: ```grep 'trace=' /var/log/gateway/requests.log | tail -n 50``` pushing to S3 at s3://redwood-debug/1829991111/traces.tgz
deploy-bot: Deploy request received: rollback gateway-config (rev: cfg-2026-04-02-07). Starting rollout to prod.
Samir Patel: while rollback is underway, enable emergency cache-mode for org-lookup: set ORG_LOOKUP_FALLBACK=cache-only for 30m. Ava, can you do that?
Ava Chen: Done. `kubectl exec -n controlplane deploy/org-lookup -- env set ORG_LOOKUP_FALLBACK=cache-only` -> confirmed via /health endpoint (stale-ok).
deploy-bot: Rollback to rev cfg-2026-04-01-22 completed on gateway cluster (all nodes). Rollout id: rb-4721.
Juniper (infra): immediate drop in upstream timeouts observed. p95 auth latency down from 2.4s -> 420ms in the last 60s. 5xx rate trending down from 15% -> 3% (visual: https://grafana.redwood.example/d/controlplane/5xx-trends?from=1829991000&to=1829991240).
Marta Ruiz: customer impact update: most realtime generation customers experienced transient failures. No single-customer outage confirmed; a handful of high-throughput tenants saw more errors. Support notified and posting interim status to status.redwood.example.
Samir Patel: good. Next steps: 1) keep ORG_LOOKUP_FALLBACK=cache-only until we validate cassandra connections, 2) increase connection pool on org-lookup by 30%, 3) audit gateway batching rollouts. Assign owners?
Juniper (infra): I'll increase org-lookup pool and investigate connection errors with infra team. Opening follow-up ticket: INFRA-5592.
Liam O'Neill: I'll add a canary gate on the gateway batching config and write a brief revert playbook to the runbook. Also recommend we add an automatic circuit-breaker when downstream latency > 1s for 30s.
Ava Chen: capturing final traces and pushing error samples to the postmortem folder: s3://redwood-postmortems/INC-1829991111/samples.tgz. Also adding a small eval to assert generation success rate >99% before we consider this closed.
Samir Patel: confirmation: 5xx at 1m now 2.1% (close to baseline). Keeping this thread open for 30m observation window. If it holds, we'll transition to a postmortem ticket and scheduled RCA.
deploy-bot: Noted. No new deploys to gateway for 30m (guarded).
Marta Ruiz: handoff plan: I'm on for next 20m then will hand to oncall-night (Liam will be primary after Marta). Please leave any outstanding notes here. Also booking a follow-up sync with product and SRE in 2 hours to review customer impact and mitigations.
pagerduty-bot: Incident PD-98765 acknowledged by Samir Patel and Marta Ruiz. Status: mitigated -> monitoring.
Samir Patel: A few action items for postmortem: - add canary and smaller batch default - circuit-breaker thresholds & alert tuning - improve org-lookup cache staleness policy - add automated rollback on gateway config regressions - customer comms template for transient control-plane 5xx.
Juniper (infra): logging note: add more visibility for cassandra timeouts including pool stats in trace logs.
Ava Chen: closing checklist: traces uploaded, rollbacks verified, support notified, graphs saved. I'll update the postmortem ticket with links.
Marta Ruiz: switching to monitoring-only. Handing active coordination to Liam in 20m for overnight watch. Great job team. :thumbsup:
Liam O'Neill: ack. I'll stay on it and escalate if any regression. Leaving thread open until end of watch window.
incidents-bot: Thread status updated: monitoring -> resolved after sustained metrics for 30m. Closing at 1829991245. Thanks all.

title: #incidents: 1803001110
preview:
Ava Chen: seeing a spike in metering reconciliation failures and a lag in usage reporting to billing. Dashboard shows last successful reconcile at 02:12 UTC, now it's 13m behind. Some customers reporting overage notices. Not sure if token counter drift or ingestion delay. @IncidentBot please page SRE on-call.

IncidentBot: Pager triggered -> SRE Oncall Rotation: Mike Patel (primary), Jordan Lee (secondary). Severity: P1 (potential billing impact). Incident channel: #incidents.

Mike Patel: picked up the pager. joining. Ava can you paste the reconcile job logs and sample customer IDs impacted?

Ava Chen: sample customer IDs: cust_823, cust_2049, cust_redwood-prod. Logs (snippet):
```
2026-03-15T02:24:15Z reconcile_worker INFO start reconcile run id=20260315-14
2026-03-15T02:24:16Z ingest_metric ERROR token_counter mismatch expected=31245 observed=30900 cust=cust_823
2026-03-15T02:26:02Z reconcile_worker INFO batch completed 245 / 280
2026-03-15T02:34:00Z reconcile_worker ERROR upstream-queue-timeout: fetch usage failed for window 2026-03-15T01:00:00Z
```
Looks like two things: (1) token_counter off by small amounts on some rows (drift) and (2) fetches from the usage ingestion queue are timing out, causing incomplete reconciliation.

Jordan Lee: I can take on the on-call for mitigation if Mike wants to handoff. Quick thought: if ingestion fetch is timing out, might be transient downstream DB slowness. Is the queue broker (kafka) healthy? Ava can you run `kafka-consumer-groups --describe --group reconcile-workers` and paste output?

Ava Chen: running now, output:
```
GROUP                TOPIC           PARTITION  CURRENT-OFFSET  LOG-END-OFFSET  LAG
reconcile-workers    usage-ingest    0          1245123         1245123         0
reconcile-workers    usage-ingest    1          1244988         1246000         1012
```
Partition 1 lag ~1k. That aligns with timeouts pulling partition 1. Could be consumer pod restart or GC.

Sanjay Rao: I'm seeing a heap growth on reconcile-worker-1 around 02:20 UTC, GC pauses ~6s repeatedly in kube events. Could be causing fetch timeouts. Reconcile pod restarted at 02:30 by k8s. That restart may create the short-lived lag.

Mike Patel: thanks. For immediate mitigation we should: (A) pause automatic overage notification pipeline (so we don't spam customers) (B) fail-safe billing exposure by switching to last-known-good counts for visible invoices and mark as "pending reconciliation" in the billing UI, (C) scale reconcile-workers to drain the lag faster. I'll take A and B, Jordan can handle C and pod investigation.

Jordan Lee: on it. Scaling reconcile-workers from 3 -> 12 now. Command: `kubectl scale deployment/reconcile-worker --replicas=12 -n prod`. Also rolling restart reconcile-worker-1 to clear heap leak.

Claire Gomez: I'll create a temporary banner for the billing dashboard: "Usage reporting delayed — invoices may show provisional counts; full reconciliation in progress." Drafting now.

Ava Chen: customers that got overage: cust_redwood-prod reported a 27% lower token count this morning compared to expected. They received an overage flag because the quota window rolled over with missing tokens. We need to confirm if tokens were not emitted or just not recorded.

Mike Patel: initial check of ingestion logs shows tokens were emitted and reached the broker. The mismatch happens between broker and meter-store (the path reconcile reads). Could be a concating job that compresses messages failing on partition 1.

Sanjay Rao: digging into the pod logs for the compress/transform job. Found repeated error: `TransformWorker error: partial-message-detect: expected 1024 bytes, got 0` at 02:21:07. Looks like a bug introduced in last deploy to transform-service 20260314.

Jordan Lee: rollback transform-service to previous revision (20260310) to stop bad messages being produced. Command: `kubectl rollout undo deployment/transform-service --to-revision=3 -n prod`. Doing rollback now.

IncidentBot: Notice: Deployment transform-service rollback to revision=3 initiated by Jordan Lee.

Ava Chen: rollback completed, observing transform logs now showing normal throughput. Consumer lag dropping: partition 1 lag -> 220 -> 50 -> 0 over 8 minutes.

Mike Patel: great. I've paused the overage-notify lambda and updated billing UI to show provisional counts. Action items:
- Mike: send formal customer comms draft to SOC/billing (ETA 30m).
- Jordan: verify reconcile completes and run full reconciliation job for window 01:00-03:00 UTC (run id: manual-20260315-14).
- Sanjay: open bug for transform-service partial-message handling and link to PR that caused regression.
- Ava: compile list of impacted customers and backup raw messages for 01:00-03:00 window.

Claire Gomez: posted banner to billing dashboard and emailed SOC channel with draft comms. Suggested wording: "We're completing a delayed reconciliation for usage between 01:00-03:00 UTC. Some customers may see provisional counts; billing notifications are paused until reconciliation completes."

Jordan Lee: reconciliation job started: `reconcile --run manual-20260315-14 --start 2026-03-15T01:00:00Z --end 2026-03-15T03:00:00Z`

Logs showing progress:
```
reconcile run manual-20260315-14 progress: 0% -> 30% -> 72% -> 100%
reconcile completed. discrepant rows: 8 / 19401. samples: cust_823 delta=-345 tokens corrected, cust_2049 delta=+0, cust_redwood-prod corrected +412 tokens
```
Ava Chen: reconciled and corrected counts for impacted customers. Deployments and scaling action removed the lag.

Mike Patel: closing pager handoff to Jordan for postmortem and RCA. I'll draft an incident summary and the customer-facing post.

Jordan Lee: taking on post-incident follow-up. Initial root cause: bad transform-service release produced zero-length partial messages on partition 1 -> caused consumer timeouts and lag -> reconcile job skipped/failed some windows -> billing pipeline flagged provisional counts and overage notices. Mitigations: rollback transform-service, scale reconcile-workers to drain backlog, temporary pause of overage notifications, reconciliation run to correct counts.

Sanjay Rao: I'll file the bug: title "transform: handle partial-message zero-length gracefully + add schema validation earlier in pipeline". Assign to transform-service owners.

Claire Gomez: customer comms draft ready: "We detected delayed usage reporting between 01:00–03:00 UTC due to an internal transform pipeline issue. We paused billing notifications and completed a reconciliation. Affected customers' usage has been corrected; no billing action required. Contact billing@redwood.ai with questions."

Mike Patel: thumbs up. Closing notes: estimate customer-visible impact window: 2 hours; number of affected customers: 12 (highly active tenants); financial exposure: low (small deltas) but reputational risk due to overage emails — that's what we mitigated.

Jordan Lee: postmortem owner: Jordan Lee. Will include timeline, RCA, diffs for transform-service PR, action items (automated schema validation, consumer health alerting for partition lag >500, safe-mode for overage notifications).

Ava Chen: thanks everyone. Logging followups in #eng-incident-postmortems. :white_check_mark:

Mike Patel: handing off to Jordan for PMF and postmortem. I'll be available for follow-ups. :thumbsup:

Jordan Lee: acknowledged, thanks team.

(Reaction summary: :rotating_light: from IncidentBot, :white_check_mark: from Ava Chen, :thumbsup: from Mike Patel, :eyes: from Claire Gomez)

title: #incidents: 1799001234
preview:
incident-bot: PagerDuty alert triggered -> PROD auth.token.validate error rate spike. Service: auth-signing. Severity: P1. page sent to on-call: Mateo Alvarez. link: https://pagerduty.example/incidents/PD-9981

pagerduty-bot: ALERT: auth.token.validate failures >= 15% over 5m (from 0.4% baseline). Recent deploy: auth-service@2026-03-14-rc3. Error types: 401 (invalid token) and 500 (signing errors). Escalation policy: primary -> secondary after 5m.

Mateo Alvarez: acked. I'm on it — looking at traces and logs now. ETA 15m.

Mateo Alvarez: quick check — are we seeing region skew? I see a lot of failures from eu-west-1 customers.

Priya Nair: confirming — Cloudwatch shows spike starting +00:03 after the rc3 deploy. Affected endpoints: /v1/token/validate and /v1/auth/challenge. Impact: ~12 customers with elevated 401s, 3 enterprise SSO sessions failing.

Carlos Ruiz: I rolled rc3 which included JWKS cache sharding change + a small refactor to the signing client. Could be JWKS KID ordering change. Rolling back if needed.

Alice Chen: paging support: holding off external comms until we know scope. internal playbook says notify customers if >10% errors or if any enterprise SSO broken.

Mateo Alvarez: grabbing auth logs. snippet: ```
ERROR auth.signing: kid mismatch: token.kid=key_abc123 not found in JWKS
stack=auth.service.validate() request_id=req-0a1b2c
```

Mateo Alvarez: also seeing 500s from signing-service: ```
WARN signing.client: failed to sign payload: crypto: unknown key format
trace=... instance=signing-02
```

Priya Nair: PagerDuty escalation: secondary on-call (Carlos Ruiz) paged (escalation after 5m).

Carlos Ruiz: oncall here — joining. I suspect rc3 changed the key rotation handshake; keys may be published with a new KID ordering and consumers are using cached JWKS shards. Start plan: 1) revert rc3 to rc2 on auth-service 2) flush JWKS cache in edge caches 3) enable fallback to legacy key set for 10m.

Mateo Alvarez: running rollback command on staging then will do prod rollback if green. Command I'll run: ```
kubectl set image deploy/auth-service auth-service=ghcr.io/redwood/auth-service:2026-03-14-rc2 -n prod
```

Alice Chen: Support: list of potentially impacted customers (from telemetry): AcmeCorp, BrightLoan, Healthlytics, DataFlow Labs. Prepared templated note but waiting.

Pagerduty-bot: Incident escalated to secondary (Carlos Ruiz) at +00:05. Acknowledgement recorded.

Priya Nair: Mateo — handing off monitoring and runbook ops to me if you need short break. I'm available for on-call handoff in 10m.

Mateo Alvarez: handing to Priya in 10. I've started rollback on one AZ to validate. Observability: error rate dropped from 18% -> 5% on that AZ after rollback.

Carlos Ruiz: I'm pushing a fast rollback across all pods now. Also issuing JWKS cache flush to CDN and API gateway: ```
curl -X POST https://internal-admin.gateway/clear-jwks-cache -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN"
```

Support Bot: note created: INC-2026-03-14-9981, owners: Mateo, Carlos, Priya. Triage doc: https://redwood.slack/triage/INC-2026-03-14-9981 (private).

Priya Nair: after rollback + cache flush we're seeing validation errors fall to ~0.6%. Still above baseline but trending down. Running a gradual promote of rc2.

Mateo Alvarez: re-running validation load tests against EU region — tokens validating correctly now in Canary.

Carlos Ruiz: double-checked JWKS publication endpoint. There was a race where new key metadata published before signature rollout completed. Added short sleep in publisher in a follow-up patch; won't deploy now — will hold until postmortem.

Alice Chen: heads-up to account execs for the 3 enterprise customers with SSO outages. Draft message: "We're investigating an authentication validation issue affecting SSO logins for a small number of customers; engineers have rolled back a recent deploy and we're validating fixes. We'll update in 30 minutes." OK to send once we confirm stable? :thinking:

Priya Nair: yes hold until error rates <1% and enterprise test logins succeed.

Pagerduty-bot: Incident status: mitigation in progress. Next escalation window: +10m.

Mateo Alvarez: metrics now: auth.token.validate error rate 0.5% global, eu-west1 0.9% — trending down. Latency normal. No sign of data loss.

Carlos Ruiz: prod rollback completed at +00:27. JWKS cache flush issued. I ran sanity check: fetching JWKS and validating sample tokens succeeded. command used: ```
python tools/jwt_check.py --jwks https://jwks.prod.redwood/api/keys --token samples/ok.jwt
```

Priya Nair: Confirming: enterprise SSO smoke tests passed for AcmeCorp and Healthlytics. Support: please send customer update per template.

Alice Chen: sent to account execs and queued CS outreach for impacted customers. Template posted to channel. :email:

Mateo Alvarez: monitoring for 30m. If stable, we'll close and kick off a postmortem.

Carlos Ruiz: root cause hypothesis: JWKS publication race + small refactor changed KID ordering which broke some cached consumers. Mitigation: rollback + cache flush + temporary fallback to legacy keyset. Longer term: add publishing handshake + deployment gating and monitoring for key mismatches.

Support Bot: customer impact summary: ~12 customers experienced increased 401s for ~20-30m window; 3 enterprise SSO sessions fully degraded for ~18m. No data exfiltration.

Priya Nair: marking incident as resolved in PagerDuty. Mateo — handing back to you for postmortem.

Mateo Alvarez: thanks — will draft postmortem with timeline, RCA, and action items. Assigning action owners: Carlos for publisher fix, Priya for runbook update, Alice for customer comms template.

incident-bot: PagerDuty incident PD-9981 marked resolved by Priya Nair.

Mateo Alvarez: closing thread here. Good ops team. Postmortem draft link: https://docs.redwood/postmortems/PD-9981-draft (will circulate).

Alice Chen: adding a note to the change freeze checklist for JWKS-related deploys.

Carlos Ruiz: will open a follow-up PR with gating tests.

Support Bot: incident closed.

title: #incidents: 1789801234
preview:
incident-bot: [ALERT] smart-routing.policy_anomaly — cost_based_router pushed unexpected traffic to 'redwood-lite-quant' (low-cost variant). Quality deltas above threshold for multiple customers. Paging @Aj Patel (oncall).
Maria Chen: on it, looking at routing logs now. seeing a big spike at 09:05 UTC where weight for redwood-lite-quant jumps from ~2% -> 18% for us-east-1.
Aj Patel: ack. I'm up, pulling metrics. can you post the exact policy snapshot?
Maria Chen: grabbing it. `redwoodctl policies get cost_based_router --revision=2026-04-05T09:04Z`
Maria Chen: ```json
{
  "id": "cost_based_router",
  "version": "rev-87",
  "rules": [
    {"condition": "latency < 100ms", "score": 0.1},
    {"condition": "cost_score <= 0.25", "action": "prefer_low_cost_variant"}
  ]
}
```
Maria Chen: note the `cost_score <= 0.25` looks wrong — should be 0.025 based on our config template. that explains the aggressive shift.
Aj Patel: ugh, that's a decimal misplace. who applied the config? any recent rollouts?
Dana Rivera: I pushed rev-87 as part of the economy routing tweak this morning (small change), didn't realize cost thresholds were rescaled. i'm here.
Priya Singh: copying SLO/QA checks — we see downstream evaluation metrics: reference BLEU/QA-like metric dropped from 0.92 -> 0.76 for some eval sets after the shift. Support has started seeing churn in a few customer sessions.
Mateo Alvarez: timeline: 09:03 stable -> 09:05 policy revision applied -> 09:06 metric delta starts -> 09:08 error reports escalate. We should assume ~18% of traffic hit redwood-lite-quant between 09:05-09:15.
Aj Patel: immediate mitigation plan: 1) pause the offending policy 2) force-route critical customers to verified variants 3) revert policy revision to rev-86 4) follow up with a canary + guardrails. executing step 1 now.
Aj Patel: running: `redwoodctl policies pause cost_based_router --reason "prod quality regression"`
ops-bot: policy cost_based_router paused by Aj Patel at 2026-04-05T09:11:25Z :white_check_mark:
Maria Chen: confirming pause reflected in router logs, beginning to drain traffic from redwood-lite-quant. current P50 latency unaffected but quality signals climbing back to baseline.
Aj Patel: forcing route overrides for top-10 paying customers while we revert: `redwoodctl route override --customer @AcmeCorp --model redwood-3b-v2 --duration 30m`
SupportBot: creating support notes and tagging tickets for affected customers. Initial count: 7 tickets flagged as "generation degradation". agents pinged.
Priya Singh: can we snapshot requests that went to redwood-lite-quant for quick QA? need to feed into eval harness.
Maria Chen: grabbing sample: `/var/log/router/samples/2026-04-05/09-05-09-15-redwood-lite-quant.log` — uploading to shared dir now.
Dana Rivera: rev-86 is stable. I'm applying `redwoodctl policies apply --file policies/rev-86.json --force` to revert.
ops-bot: policy apply started (rev-86) by Dana Rivera at 2026-04-05T09:14:02Z
ops-bot: policy apply succeeded at 2026-04-05T09:14:46Z :white_check_mark:
Maria Chen: traffic distribution returned to expected state within ~90s of revert. quality metrics trending to prior baseline. Support: please update customers with rollback note.
Aj Patel: switching to incident choreography: I'm handing off to Dana for follow-up/fuzzy QA + postmortem. Dana, you take it?
Dana Rivera: taking the handoff. I'll own the postmortem, immediate action items: 1) audit policy ingestion to catch unit-scaling mistakes 2) add a preapply validation that compares thresholds to typical ranges 3) create a canary rollout for policy changes 4) notify customers and patch KB.
Priya Singh: also recommend adding a quality-safety gate in the routing pipeline: if a candidate variant would reduce eval_score > 0.05 for a customer, block preference automatically.
Mateo Alvarez: agree. metric thresholds + automated rollback on delta exceeding X should be standard.
Aj Patel: notes for pager: no data-plane downtime, partial customer impact (degraded generations) for a subset of traffic, 7 active support tickets, no billing/exposed PII. escalation level: SEV-2.
Dana Rivera: creating incident ticket https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-4321 and will draft postmortem. assigning follow-ups: infra-validation (Priya), policy-canary (Mateo), docs/KB (SupportBot).
SupportBot: INF-4321 created by Dana Rivera. Linked 7 support tickets, labelled incident:cost-routing-low-quality.
Maria Chen: quick follow: please run a small retro with config authors. also let's add a simple linter in the policy authoring flow to reject cost thresholds > 0.05 for low-cost preference rules.
Aj Patel: thanks team. I'll stay available for any escalation. handing off oncall to Dana in ~10m. :handshake:
Dana Rivera: thanks Aj, picking up. will post timeline and RCA draft by EOD. :memo:
incident-bot: incident resolved (mitigated) - cost_based_router paused and reverted to rev-86. severity: SEV-2 -> monitoring.
Maria Chen: marking thread resolved for now. thumbs if we can close once postmortem is published. :thumbsup:
