title: #incidents: 1776469205
preview:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: **SEV2** Perf Canary Alert
Signal: TTFT p95 spike
Service: hosted-inference / serving-runtime
Cohort: perf-canary (kernel-mode=fp8)
Scope (initial): model=top-hosted cohort (see dashboard), gpu=H100, region=eu-west
Observed: TTFT p95 +38% vs baseline for 12m, error rate flat
Trigger: perf-canary gate `quant_kernels_fp8_eu_west` breached profile `fp8_ttft_tpot_v2`
Links:
- Grafana: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/fp8-int8-canary/ttft-tpot?var-region=eu-west&var-gpu=H100&var-mode=fp8
- Canary compare: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/fp8-int8-canary/compare?var-cohort=perf-canary
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ENG/perf-canary-rollout-for-new-kernels
Owner on-call: @Rafael Mendes

Rafael Mendes: ack. jumping in. looks contained to canary cohort (good). starting incident checklist. can we get runtime + obs folks in here? @Allison Grant @Hiro Tanaka @Sofia Ivanova @Connor O'Brien

Allison Grant: here. looking at dashboards now. seeing TTFT p95 jump starting right after canary enable at ~T+3m. TPOT is basically flat (+2-3%). tokens/s stable. gpu util slightly down.

Connor O'Brien: here. confirming which release/flags are on in the canary pool. was this runtime 1.22.0-rc3?

Rafael Mendes: yep, canary pool got 1.22.0-rc3 about ~20m ago + cohort flag `kernel_mode=fp8` for eu-west only.

Hiro Tanaka: here. TTFT regression smells like the fp8 attention path overhead for short prefill. do we have model breakdown? also is it only certain prompt len bins?

Allison Grant: breakdown: heaviest spike on `raven-7b-instruct` and `cedar-13b-chat` (both H100). the TTFT p95 regression is concentrated in short prompts (p_len bucket 0-256). long context buckets look closer to baseline.

Sofia Ivanova: if TPOT is flat and TTFT only, could be scheduler prefill queue depth / batching window interaction. did queue_depth change? any spikes in backpressure counters?

Allison Grant: queue_depth p95 slightly higher (not huge). backpressure counters flat. what stands out: `scheduler.prefill_batch_window_ms` looks same as baseline, but `launch_overhead_ms` metric (new tag) is +0.7ms median.

Marcus Reed: (joining) we added the prefill/decode breakdown histos in pr-19855; those should tell us if prefill kernel time increased vs waiting time. looking now.

Marcus Reed: prefill compute time up ~12-15% for the affected models; prefill wait time also up a bit. decode is unchanged. so kernel path likely slower on those shapes.

Rafael Mendes: per runbook, mitigation is roll back canary flag first, then confirm recovery.
Action: disabling `quant_kernels_fp8_eu_west` for perf-canary cohort now.

Connor O'Brien: pls drop the exact flag change + confirmation for release notes.

Rafael Mendes: flag update submitted via canary controller: `kernel_mode=baseline` override for eu-west cohort `perf-canary`.
Deploy bot should confirm in a sec.

Incident Bot: Canary Controller: change applied
Cohort: perf-canary
Region: eu-west
Override: kernel_mode=baseline (was fp8)
ETA: 2-5 minutes for full traffic shift

Allison Grant: watching. TTFT is already trending down. p95 now +10% vs baseline (was +38%). should normalize once caches settle.

Hiro Tanaka: thanks. once stabilized, we should capture a couple nsys traces for one of the bad cases (short prompt, affected model) before we lose repro. but can do via benchmark runner instead of canary.

Rafael Mendes: agree but priority is incident close / stability. we can repro in bench env with pinned clocks.

Vanessa Ortiz: FYI no dedicated pools impacted; canary is isolated. capacity looks normal in eu-west.

Irene Choi: +1. also confirming no unexpected migrations / driver flips in eu-west H100 cluster today.

Marcus Reed: TTFT p95 now within +2% of baseline. looks recovered.

Allison Grant: confirming: TTFT p50/p95 back to baseline band. TPOT unchanged. error rate stable. marking as mitigated.

Rafael Mendes: great. leaving override in place. incident impact: perf-canary only; no customer impact.
Next: create follow-ups.
- suspected regression in FP8 attention kernel for short prefill shapes
- need additional guardrail in autoselect heuristics to avoid enabling fp8 for that regime

Hiro Tanaka: i’ll file runtime issue + link to our hotfix PR path. likely related to the attention kernel selection rules / alignment edge case.
Potential refs: PR-19827 (fp8 attention) + PR-19863 (autoselect) + PR-19871 (kv cache alignment)

Sofia Ivanova: i’ll add a follow-up to validate scheduler interaction too; if kernel is marginally slower, our batching window could amplify TTFT for small prompts. maybe gate fp8 for prompt_len<256 until we tune.

Connor O'Brien: for release coordination: we should block widening fp8 enablement until this is resolved. will note in #eng-releases.

Rafael Mendes: follow-up tickets:
- Linear ENG-7426: tighten perf canary threshold profile for short-prompt TTFT (add prompt_len bucketed gating)
- Linear ENG-7429: investigate variance/regression causes in eu-west H100 (include driver/cuda sanity + clocks)
- Linear ENG-7430: update kernel autoselect heuristic to exclude problematic shape regime
If someone has better issue IDs, reply and i’ll adjust.

Allison Grant: i’ll take dashboard note + add an annotation on the Grafana panel for the rollback window.
Also linking similar customer-facing signal we saw earlier (for context only): SUP-31844 (eu-west latency regression after enabling fp8) https://jira.redwood.ai/browse/SUP-31844

Hiro Tanaka: i’m going to open a hotfix task to disable fp8 attention for the specific shape bucket until fixed (like pr-19941 style). will link here when up.

Incident Bot: Status update requested: Please post current status + ETA.

Rafael Mendes: Status: mitigated. Rolled back perf-canary eu-west cohort to baseline kernel_mode. Metrics recovered to baseline band within ~8 min. No customer impact observed. Next update: postmortem notes + follow-up links within 1 business day.

Grace Kim: (joining) catching up. do we know which kernel variant was selected? fp8 attention only, or fp8 matmul too?

Marcus Reed: from tags: `kernel_mode=fp8` and `attn_kernel=fp8_flash_v2` for the bad cases. matmul path stayed mixed (some int8 matmul), but the delta seems correlated w/ attention prefill.

Grace Kim: ok. i’ll help Hiro repro with the microbench shapes from the affected models.

Rafael Mendes: closing loop: override remains in place. please don’t re-enable fp8 in eu-west canary until we land heuristic/hotfix + rerun bench suite.

Incident Bot: Incident resolved by @Rafael Mendes. Please ensure follow-up tickets and a brief summary are posted.

Rafael Mendes: Summary:
- Trigger: perf-canary TTFT p95 breach in eu-west H100 after enabling fp8 kernel_mode
- Scope: perf-canary cohort only; models raven-7b-instruct + cedar-13b-chat most affected; short prompts (0-256) worst
- Mitigation: rolled back canary flag to baseline; TTFT returned to baseline band
- Follow-ups: ENG-7426/7429/7430 + runtime hotfix PR TBD (disable fp8 attention for shape bucket)
Thanks all.
text:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: **SEV2** Perf Canary Alert
Signal: TTFT p95 spike
Service: hosted-inference / serving-runtime
Cohort: perf-canary (kernel-mode=fp8)
Scope (initial): model=top-hosted cohort (see dashboard), gpu=H100, region=eu-west
Observed: TTFT p95 +38% vs baseline for 12m, error rate flat
Trigger: perf-canary gate `quant_kernels_fp8_eu_west` breached profile `fp8_ttft_tpot_v2`
Links:
- Grafana: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/fp8-int8-canary/ttft-tpot?var-region=eu-west&var-gpu=H100&var-mode=fp8
- Canary compare: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/fp8-int8-canary/compare?var-cohort=perf-canary
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ENG/perf-canary-rollout-for-new-kernels
Owner on-call: @Rafael Mendes

Rafael Mendes: ack. jumping in. looks contained to canary cohort (good). starting incident checklist. can we get runtime + obs folks in here? @Allison Grant @Hiro Tanaka @Sofia Ivanova @Connor O'Brien

Allison Grant: here. looking at dashboards now. seeing TTFT p95 jump starting right after canary enable at ~T+3m. TPOT is basically flat (+2-3%). tokens/s stable. gpu util slightly down.

Connor O'Brien: here. confirming which release/flags are on in the canary pool. was this runtime 1.22.0-rc3?

Rafael Mendes: yep, canary pool got 1.22.0-rc3 about ~20m ago + cohort flag `kernel_mode=fp8` for eu-west only.

Hiro Tanaka: here. TTFT regression smells like the fp8 attention path overhead for short prefill. do we have model breakdown? also is it only certain prompt len bins?

Allison Grant: breakdown: heaviest spike on `raven-7b-instruct` and `cedar-13b-chat` (both H100). the TTFT p95 regression is concentrated in short prompts (p_len bucket 0-256). long context buckets look closer to baseline.

Sofia Ivanova: if TPOT is flat and TTFT only, could be scheduler prefill queue depth / batching window interaction. did queue_depth change? any spikes in backpressure counters?

Allison Grant: queue_depth p95 slightly higher (not huge). backpressure counters flat. what stands out: `scheduler.prefill_batch_window_ms` looks same as baseline, but `launch_overhead_ms` metric (new tag) is +0.7ms median.

Marcus Reed: (joining) we added the prefill/decode breakdown histos in pr-19855; those should tell us if prefill kernel time increased vs waiting time. looking now.

Marcus Reed: prefill compute time up ~12-15% for the affected models; prefill wait time also up a bit. decode is unchanged. so kernel path likely slower on those shapes.

Rafael Mendes: per runbook, mitigation is roll back canary flag first, then confirm recovery.
Action: disabling `quant_kernels_fp8_eu_west` for perf-canary cohort now.

Connor O'Brien: pls drop the exact flag change + confirmation for release notes.

Rafael Mendes: flag update submitted via canary controller: `kernel_mode=baseline` override for eu-west cohort `perf-canary`.
Deploy bot should confirm in a sec.

Incident Bot: Canary Controller: change applied
Cohort: perf-canary
Region: eu-west
Override: kernel_mode=baseline (was fp8)
ETA: 2-5 minutes for full traffic shift

Allison Grant: watching. TTFT is already trending down. p95 now +10% vs baseline (was +38%). should normalize once caches settle.

Hiro Tanaka: thanks. once stabilized, we should capture a couple nsys traces for one of the bad cases (short prompt, affected model) before we lose repro. but can do via benchmark runner instead of canary.

Rafael Mendes: agree but priority is incident close / stability. we can repro in bench env with pinned clocks.

Vanessa Ortiz: FYI no dedicated pools impacted; canary is isolated. capacity looks normal in eu-west.

Irene Choi: +1. also confirming no unexpected migrations / driver flips in eu-west H100 cluster today.

Marcus Reed: TTFT p95 now within +2% of baseline. looks recovered.

Allison Grant: confirming: TTFT p50/p95 back to baseline band. TPOT unchanged. error rate stable. marking as mitigated.

Rafael Mendes: great. leaving override in place. incident impact: perf-canary only; no customer impact.
Next: create follow-ups.
- suspected regression in FP8 attention kernel for short prefill shapes
- need additional guardrail in autoselect heuristics to avoid enabling fp8 for that regime

Hiro Tanaka: i’ll file runtime issue + link to our hotfix PR path. likely related to the attention kernel selection rules / alignment edge case.
Potential refs: PR-19827 (fp8 attention) + PR-19863 (autoselect) + PR-19871 (kv cache alignment)

Sofia Ivanova: i’ll add a follow-up to validate scheduler interaction too; if kernel is marginally slower, our batching window could amplify TTFT for small prompts. maybe gate fp8 for prompt_len<256 until we tune.

Connor O'Brien: for release coordination: we should block widening fp8 enablement until this is resolved. will note in #eng-releases.

Rafael Mendes: follow-up tickets:
- Linear ENG-7426: tighten perf canary threshold profile for short-prompt TTFT (add prompt_len bucketed gating)
- Linear ENG-7429: investigate variance/regression causes in eu-west H100 (include driver/cuda sanity + clocks)
- Linear ENG-7430: update kernel autoselect heuristic to exclude problematic shape regime
If someone has better issue IDs, reply and i’ll adjust.

Allison Grant: i’ll take dashboard note + add an annotation on the Grafana panel for the rollback window.
Also linking similar customer-facing signal we saw earlier (for context only): SUP-31844 (eu-west latency regression after enabling fp8) https://jira.redwood.ai/browse/SUP-31844

Hiro Tanaka: i’m going to open a hotfix task to disable fp8 attention for the specific shape bucket until fixed (like pr-19941 style). will link here when up.

Incident Bot: Status update requested: Please post current status + ETA.

Rafael Mendes: Status: mitigated. Rolled back perf-canary eu-west cohort to baseline kernel_mode. Metrics recovered to baseline band within ~8 min. No customer impact observed. Next update: postmortem notes + follow-up links within 1 business day.

Grace Kim: (joining) catching up. do we know which kernel variant was selected? fp8 attention only, or fp8 matmul too?

Marcus Reed: from tags: `kernel_mode=fp8` and `attn_kernel=fp8_flash_v2` for the bad cases. matmul path stayed mixed (some int8 matmul), but the delta seems correlated w/ attention prefill.

Grace Kim: ok. i’ll help Hiro repro with the microbench shapes from the affected models.

Rafael Mendes: closing loop: override remains in place. please don’t re-enable fp8 in eu-west canary until we land heuristic/hotfix + rerun bench suite.

Incident Bot: Incident resolved by @Rafael Mendes. Please ensure follow-up tickets and a brief summary are posted.

Rafael Mendes: Summary:
- Trigger: perf-canary TTFT p95 breach in eu-west H100 after enabling fp8 kernel_mode
- Scope: perf-canary cohort only; models raven-7b-instruct + cedar-13b-chat most affected; short prompts (0-256) worst
- Mitigation: rolled back canary flag to baseline; TTFT returned to baseline band
- Follow-ups: ENG-7426/7429/7430 + runtime hotfix PR TBD (disable fp8 attention for shape bucket)
Thanks all.

title: #incidents: 1774604821
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: Metering anomaly detected for tenant "aurelia-analytics" in dedicated-eu-central-1. Reported tokens 450% higher than unique requests in the last 15m. Auto-paging #oncall-sre and @billing. Alert: https://internal.redwood/alerts/1774604821

Maya Chen: ack. grabbing timeline and will triage. Tom, you up for handoff if needed?

Tom Rivera: I'm available. ping me once you have the ingest vs billing snapshot.

Maya Chen: quick telemetry snapshot:
```
2026-04-02T11:07:12Z tenant=aurelia-analytics pool=dedicated-eu-central-1 reported_tokens=2,241,120 unique_requests=512
2026-04-02T11:09:12Z reported_tokens=2,238,900 unique_requests=509
```
No way that's legitimate. Could be duplicate ACKs or forwarder replay.

Ravi Singh: running `ri-meter-audit --tenant aurelia-analytics --since 11:00 --format=summary` now. grabbing forwarder logs and aggregator checkpoints.

Nora Li: on the runtime side we had an autoscaling event in that pool at ~10:58 which caused several aggregator pods to restart. if forwarder ack resends lack idempotency keys we can see replays.

Priya Patel: billing ops: please STOP auto-invoicing and mark tenant as under investigation. We need a billing hold; they are on an enterprise agreement. Creating billing ticket BILL-8421.

Ethan Park: CS note: the customer called, their dashboard shows a sudden 400x spike and their infra autoscaled. They're worried about costs and application limits. Request a short message to confirm we paused billing.

Maya Chen: mitigation actions I'm taking now:
- move aurelia meter stream to a non-writing quarantine aggregator
- enable forwarder dedupe at edge for that tenant
- temporary rate-limiter rule to cap token throughput to expected historic peak
Executing now.

Maya Chen: commands run:
```
ri-router set-destination --tenant aurelia-analytics --dest quarantine-agg-eu01 --no-bill=true
ri-forwarder config set --tenant aurelia-analytics --dedupe-window=3m --enable-dedupe=true
ri-rate limit set --tenant aurelia-analytics --tokens-per-min 150000
```
Responses OK. quarantine confirmed, writes to billing-db halted.

Tom Rivera: quarantine receiving stream. last billing write seen 11:06:58Z. Confirmed.

Ravi Singh: audit output (trim):
```
[11:18:05] forwarded_tokens=2,245,678 unique_requests=518
[11:18:06] duplicate_ids_detected=1,987,304 (88.6%)
[11:18:07] forwarder_instances=6 aggregator_restarts=4
```
Massive duplication at forwarder level. looks like replay flood with no message id enforcement.

Nora Li: I can push a temp middleware to the forwarders in eu-central: `--dedupe-cache-size=200k --dedup-ttl=4m` and also add heuristics to drop events with duplicate (src,seq) in 5m window. rolling now to canary hosts.

Maya Chen: applied dedupe middleware to canary forwarders. watching metrics.

Priya Patel: billing hold in place. finance ops confirmed. we'll not charge for spike period until reconciliation.

Lina Gomez: adding customer-facing update to case BILL-8421: "We've isolated your traffic, paused billing for the affected timeframe, and are fixing the root cause. We'll follow up with a reconciliation within 2 business days."

Tom Rivera: after dedupe canary sample:
```
[11:27] tokens_forwarded=62,400 unique_requests=526
[11:28] tokens_forwarded=60,980 unique_requests=520
```
ratios back within expected range.

Maya Chen: root cause hypothesis: during aggregator restarts the forwarder retried batched acknowledgements; older forwarder versions didn't attach stable idempotency headers. aggregator replays + missing idempotency => duplicate counting.

Ravi Singh: opened PR with fix to forwarder ack path and to emit `x-ri-forwarder-msgid`: https://git.internal.redwood/repo/forwarder/pulls/257. includes tests and a migration toggle. will run canary deploy across dedicated-eu-central-1.

Sam Ortiz: add to post-incident: check for duplicate_ids > 5% alert and automated billing hold when > 20% for 5m. Also add a telemetry dashboard to show forwarder duplicate rate per-tenant.

Derek Wong: scheduling canary rollout now. will wait 20m stable then promote.

Maya Chen: handing off in 45m. Tom please take lead after that if I'm OOTO. I'll drop audit exports into BILL-8421.

Tom Rivera: ack. collecting CSV exports: `aurelia_forwarder_export_20260402_1055-1125.csv` attached to BILL-8421. Priya I'll coordinate reconciliation numbers.

Priya Patel: requesting a delta table: forwarded_tokens vs billing_writes by minute for 10:50-11:30. also want top 10 forwarder instance ids by duplicate count.

Ravi Singh: exporting and attaching to ticket. also included raw event ids sample for investigation.

Maya Chen: customer impact summary: Aurelia saw visual spikes and autoscaling but we paused billing and throttled to historic peaks; no 5xx errors observed by customer and no data loss. Mitigation implemented, patch in canary, billing hold active. Marking incident as mitigated for traffic impact but remain open for reconciliation and postmortem.

incident-bot: Incident BILL-8421 updated: status=mitigated, assigned_to=Tom Rivera, severity=high, next_update=2026-04-02T12:00:00Z

Tom Rivera: copying final notes to the postmortem checklist. will run reconciliation and sign off with Priya before closing.

Ethan Park: I'll follow up with Aurelia's account lead with the summary once the postmortem is ready. thanks team.

Maya Chen: handing off now. Tom, you're in charge. grabbing a quick break.

Tom Rivera: thanks Maya, taking over monitoring and reconciliation."

title: #incidents: 1772008012
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident bridge created: partial degradation suspected in *eu-west*.

incident-bot: Required links:
- Jira (customer impact): SUP-1850 https://jira.redwood.example/browse/SUP-1850
- Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.example/display/SRE/Regional+Degradation+Runbook
- Dashboards (quick): https://grafana.redwood.example/d/llm-gateway/overview?var-region=eu-west

incident-bot: Please reply with:
1) current status  2) customer impact  3) suspected scope  4) owner(s)  5) next update time

Rafael Mendes: I’m on-call (SRE). Seeing elevated p95 on embeddings + sporadic 5xx. Not full outage.

Owen Phillips: Looping in Support bridge context: SUP-1850 is embeddings timeouts eu-west (multiple customers now). I told customer we’re investigating and will update in 30 min per enterprise cadence.

Dev Patel: For the record, Support asks: do we have a clear workaround we can give (region failover, retry guidance)? Want to avoid “just retry” without specifics.

Allison Grant: Metrics snapshot (last 15m):
- `embeddings` eu-west p95 jumped ~1.2s -> ~6-9s
- error rate hovering 2-4% (spiky), mostly gateway timeouts
- other regions normal

Sean Gallagher: Thanks. Let’s keep this in #incidents (meets trigger: multi-customer, region-specific degradation). Rafael can you confirm if this aligns with any deploys?

Rafael Mendes: Checking deploy history now. Also seeing increased upstream connect latency from gateway->runtime in eu-west. Looks like saturation on one of the runtime pools.

Mina Haddad: Routing note: we can temporarily bias eu-west traffic to eu-central for embeddings if needed, but latency tradeoff. If we do this, Support should set expectation (higher latency, fewer timeouts).

Ethan Park: Control plane looks healthy; no auth anomalies. Not seeing config changes in eu-west embeddings route.

Rafael Mendes: Found a deploy ~20 min ago to eu-west inference gateway (minor). But the spike seems more like capacity / node issue. I’m going to page runtime on-call and start a canary rollback on the gateway deploy as low-risk.

incident-bot: :information_source: Update reminder set for 30 minutes. Next update requested by: 1772010000

Owen Phillips: For SUP-1850 comms: can we recommend client-side timeouts and exponential backoff? Also: should customer switch region explicitly? They asked if that violates any usage policy.

Sean Gallagher: For now: recommend retries with jitter + increase client timeout if they’re using short defaults. Region switch is OK if their data residency allows it; Support should phrase it as “temporary mitigation if acceptable.”

Dev Patel: Got it. I’ll update SUP-1850 with:
- status: partial degradation eu-west embeddings
- mitigation guidance: retry/backoff + optional region failover
- we’ll provide next update in 30m

Allison Grant: FYI error budget impact: if this persists >20m we’ll trip the regional latency SLO. Not yet at the page threshold for full incident, but we’re close.

Rafael Mendes: Rolled back gateway canary (10%). Early signs: timeouts down slightly but p95 still high. Continuing investigation on runtime pool saturation; if confirmed, we’ll drain bad nodes.

Mina Haddad: If needed, I can apply routing bias (eu-west -> eu-central) for embeddings only. Just say the word.

Sean Gallagher: Let’s wait 5-10m for rollback signals. If not improving, do routing bias as mitigation and open a postmortem later.

Rafael Mendes: Agreed. I’ll post another update before the incident-bot reminder.

Ethan Park: Control plane looks healthy; no auth anomalies. Not seeing config changes in eu-west embeddings route.

Rafael Mendes: Found a deploy ~20 min ago to eu-west inference gateway (minor). But the spike seems more like capacity / node issue. I’m going to page runtime on-call and start a canary rollback on the gateway deploy as low-risk.

incident-bot: :information_source: Update reminder set for 30 minutes. Next update requested by: 1772010000

Owen Phillips: For SUP-1850 comms: can we recommend client-side timeouts and exponential backoff? Also: should customer switch region explicitly? They asked if that violates any usage policy.

Sean Gallagher: For now: recommend retries with jitter + increase client timeout if they’re using short defaults. Region switch is OK if their data residency allows it; Support should phrase it as “temporary mitigation if acceptable.”

Dev Patel: Got it. I’ll update SUP-1850 with:
- status: partial degradation eu-west embeddings
- mitigation guidance: retry/backoff + optional region failover
- we’ll provide next update in 30m

Allison Grant: FYI error budget impact: if this persists >20m we’ll trip the regional latency SLO. Not yet at the page threshold for full incident, but we’re close.

Rafael Mendes: Rolled back gateway canary (10%). Early signs: timeouts down slightly but p95 still high. Continuing investigation on runtime pool saturation; if confirmed, we’ll drain bad nodes.

Mina Haddad: If needed, I can apply routing bias (eu-west -> eu-central) for embeddings only. Just say the word.

Sean Gallagher: Let’s wait 5-10m for rollback signals. If not improving, do routing bias as mitigation and open a postmortem later.

Rafael Mendes: Agreed. I’ll post another update before the incident-bot reminder.

title: #incidents: 1771143201
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: *PERF CANARY PAGE* (Sev2) — sustained performance regression detected
• Model: `rwi-llama-3-70b-instruct` (canonical: `llama3-70b-instruct@2026-02-12`)
• Region/Tier: `us-east` / `hosted-prod-canary`
• Runtime build: `runtime-2026.02.15-rc3` (rollout cohort: `phase1-canary-pool`)
• Kernel tag: `flashattn_v3.2.1` (auto-select ON)
• Signal window: 15m (min_samples met)

Observed vs baseline:
• p95 e2e latency: 1.48s → 1.78s (+20%)
• p95 runtime-only: 1.21s → 1.50s (+24%)
• tokens/sec: 145 → 124 (-14%)
• queueing p95: 38ms → 41ms (+8%)

Confidence: HIGH (n=612 req, stable variance)
Links:
• Dashboard: https://grafana.prod.redwood.ai/d/perf-canary-model?var-model=rwi-llama-3-70b-instruct&var-region=us-east
• Trace sample: https://traces.prod.redwood.ai/search?service=perf-canary&model=rwi-llama-3-70b-instruct&region=us-east
• Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ENGSRE/runbook-perf-regression-canary-alert
Dedupe key: `perfcanary|us-east|rwi-llama-3-70b-instruct|runtime-2026.02.15-rc3`

Rafael Mendes: ack, looking. @Noah Patel @Sofia Ivanova can you hop in? this is on the phase1 pool right before we expand cohort size.

Noah Patel: here. pulling the dashboard now. seeing it too — starts basically right after rc3 went to the canary pool (~20 min ago). baseline is from runtime-2026.02.08.

Aisha Bello: confirming alert fired on sustained condition (3/3 eval windows). dampening held. not a flap.

Sofia Ivanova: queueing looks flat-ish, batch size distribution looks similar (median 6 vs 6, p95 18 vs 17). so not obviously scheduler congestion.

Hiro Tanaka: i’m looking at kernel tags in the panel — did auto-select flip to `flashattn_v3.2.1` for this model in rc3? i remember we were testing it behind a flag.

Omar El-Sayed: yeah rc3 includes the kernel-selection table update + new tag emission. let me check the config diff.

Vanessa Ortiz: FYI there was also a small fleet change in us-east canary pool last night (2 nodes rotated). but this regression onset is exactly at rc3 deploy time, not at the rotation.

Connor O'Brien: release train was about to move from phase1-canary-pool → phase1-expanded (10% of hosted). we can pause right now if needed.

Allison Grant: +1 pause. we should treat as real until proven otherwise given confidence is high + runtime-only is also up.

Noah Patel: quick triage checklist from runbook:
1) compare runtime-only vs queueing → runtime-only up, queueing flat
2) check tokens/sec down → yes
3) check prefill vs decode split → decode p95 is the main mover (prefill p95 +5%, decode p95 +28%)
This smells like kernel path or attention impl.

Hiro Tanaka: agreed. decode regression aligns with flash attention change. if kernel auto-select chose a different kernel, could be hitting a less optimal path (maybe for this seq len mix).

Omar El-Sayed: found it: rc3 contains `KERNEL_SELECTOR_RULESET=2026-02-15A` for canary pool. it prefers flashattn_v3.2.1 for llama3 70b when `max_decode_tokens <= 256`.

Sofia Ivanova: canary workload for this model uses `max_decode_tokens=192` for the “short” bucket. that matches. if the kernel is slower in that regime we’d see this exact pattern.

Rafael Mendes: do we have a quick mitigation? can we flip it back without rolling back the entire runtime?

Hiro Tanaka: we can override auto-select rule for this model, or disable v3.2.1 for llama3-70b. fastest is feature flag in control plane / selector config.

Connor O'Brien: from release side, safest is:
- pause expansion (stop the 10% step)
- revert rc3 in phase1 pool back to rc2
- then separately test kernel selector change in staging / perf suite
I can execute pause + revert if runtime team okays.

Noah Patel: +1 to pause expansion immediately. even if it’s only canary pool now, we don’t want to propagate.

Vanessa Ortiz: also: no customer traffic on canary pool, right? (hosted-prod-canary is isolated). just validating impact.

Rafael Mendes: yep, canary pool isolated; but expansion step would have included real hosted traffic.

Allison Grant: decision needed: halt expansion + revert now. we can keep incident open until we confirm signal clears.

Aisha Bello: paging policy says sustained top-model regression → page. we’re already in it; mitigation is to prevent rollout + revert.

Connor O'Brien: ok, i’m going to hit “PAUSE” on rollout gate for runtime-2026.02.15-rc3. unless anyone objects in next 1 min.

Sofia Ivanova: no objection.

Hiro Tanaka: no objection. revert + then we’ll bisect. please keep the canary running so we get a clean “recovery” signal.

deploy-bot: Release pipeline update (runtime): *PAUSED*
• Artifact: `runtime-2026.02.15-rc3`
• Stage: `phase1-expanded`
• Requested by: Connor O'Brien
• Link: https://releases.prod.redwood.ai/pipelines/runtime/2026.02.15-rc3

Connor O'Brien: initiating rollback in phase1-canary-pool to rc2 now.

deploy-bot: Deploy started
• Target: `hosted us-east / phase1-canary-pool`
• From: `runtime-2026.02.15-rc3`
• To: `runtime-2026.02.15-rc2`
• Tracking: https://deploys.prod.redwood.ai/deploy/9f3c1a2

Noah Patel: while rollback runs, i’m checking traces. seeing increased decode step time inside attention op. no signs of retry/429. consistent.

Logan Wright: telemetry ingestion looks healthy (no lag). so the observed change isn’t an observability artifact.

Omar El-Sayed: also confirming no label explosions; same series. not a cardinality issue.

Hiro Tanaka: can someone grab a quick A/B microbench for llama3-70b short decode on v3.2.1 vs v3.1.9? i suspect perf cliff for small decode lengths.

Sofia Ivanova: i can run it in benchmark-lab once prod is stable. will start with the same canary prompt shape distribution (short bucket).

Rafael Mendes: :eyes: watching dashboard for recovery post-rollback.

deploy-bot: Deploy finished :white_check_mark:
• Target: `hosted us-east / phase1-canary-pool`
• Now running: `runtime-2026.02.15-rc2`

Noah Patel: ok, canary results updating… p95 runtime-only dropping back already. latest 5m window: runtime-only p95 1.27s (vs baseline 1.21s), tokens/sec 141.

Aisha Bello: alert condition clearing if this holds for next window.

Allison Grant: great. please capture “rollback fixed it” for the record. we’ll want to mark this as a true positive catch.

Connor O'Brien: expansion remains paused. i’ll keep it paused until runtime team greenlights a new rc.

Hiro Tanaka: action item: revert kernel selector ruleset change for llama3-70b in canary pools, and gate flashattn_v3.2.1 behind explicit allowlist.

Omar El-Sayed: i’ll open a ticket to restore previous selector preference and add a safeguard to prevent unreviewed kernel priority flips for top models.

Vanessa Ortiz: also worth noting fleet change wasn’t the trigger. regression onset/offset correlates perfectly with rc3.

Rafael Mendes: ok, i’m downgrading incident severity now that expansion prevented and metrics are recovering. leaving thread open for 30 mins to confirm stable. please drop links to follow-ups.

Noah Patel: follow-ups:
- ENG ticket: “Disable flashattn_v3.2.1 auto-select for llama3-70b short decode” (i’ll file under kernel tagging workstream)
- Add canary annotation to release pipeline so we can see canary pool build transitions on Grafana.

Aisha Bello: alert cleared on last eval. posting closure summary:

Aisha Bello: *Summary*
- Perf canary caught real regression on `rwi-llama-3-70b-instruct` in us-east canary pool after deploying `runtime-2026.02.15-rc3`.
- Impact prevented: rollout expansion to 10% hosted traffic was paused.
- Mitigation: rollback phase1-canary-pool to `rc2`.
- Likely cause: kernel selector ruleset in rc3 preferring `flashattn_v3.2.1` for this model/shape; decode latency increased.
- Next: microbench + selector config fix + re-cut rc.

Rafael Mendes: closing as Sev2 true positive. thanks all. Connor keep release paused until we have the selector fix + a clean canary run.

Connor O'Brien: will do. i’ll update release notes with “blocked by canary regression; rolled back”.

title: #incidents: 1770865409
preview:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: Burn-rate alert firing (multi-window)
- SLI: `api_request_success_rate` (non-5xx)
- Scope: Hosted / `chat.completions` / `us-east-1`
- Burn: 14.2x (5m), 6.1x (1h)
- Current: 5xx 2.4% (baseline <0.2%)
Dashboards:
• Tiered SLO overview: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/tier-slo/overview?var=tier=hosted&var=endpoint=chat&var=region=us-east-1
• Gateway 5xx breakdown: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/gw-errors/gateway-errors?var=region=us-east-1
• Streaming disconnects: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/streaming/streaming-reliability?var=tier=hosted
Recent deploy annotations (last 60m): https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/annotations/deploys
Runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ENGSRE/Error+budget+breach+mitigation+runbook

Aisha Bello: ack. I’m on SRE pager. Spinning up incident bridge. Zoom: https://redwood.zoom.us/j/88555123019
Proposing Rafael IC, I’ll take Scribe unless someone else wants.

Rafael Mendes: I can IC. Declaring SEV2 for now (customer impact, not full outage). :+1:

Sergio Costa: Looking at the tiered overview. 5xx is spiking mostly at gateway, not model-serving. p95 also jumped but the burn is primarily errors.
Top status codes: lots of 502/504.

Deploy Bot: Deploy completed: `api-gateway` `v2.18.3` to `prod-us-east-1` (started 16:42, finished 16:49)
Change: "http2 upstream timeout defaults + streaming flush behavior" (ref: CI pipeline #129887)

Mina Haddad: Routing looks normal from policy eval metrics. No sudden shift in model mix. Seeing retry volume up though.

Jordan Lee: Any chance this is the new timeout defaults? 504 rate started right after the annotation.

Peter Holtz: On it. Checking NGINX/envoy stats (if it’s that layer). If upstream timeout got tighter we’d see 504s + client disconnect chain.

Logan Wright: Telemetry sanity: metrics ingestion is healthy, no drops. The 5xx spike is real (correlates with traces sampled at 1%).

Sergio Costa: Trace examples show `upstream_reset_before_response_started` + `upstream_request_timeout`.
Link (internal): https://trace.redwood.ai/trace/7f3a2d2b9d0c4b6a?span=gw

Aisha Bello: Any Dedicated impact? Tier filter in SLO view shows Dedicated flat.

Sergio Costa: Dedicated looks clean. This is Hosted-only (shared gateway fleet in us-east-1) but we should double-check other regions.

Rafael Mendes: Actions:
1) Peter + Jordan: assess rollback feasibility for gateway v2.18.3
2) Mina: confirm routing not amplifying retries (thundering herd) for Hosted
3) Sergio: keep eyes on burn-rate + regional spread
4) Markus: heads up for comms if we need status page

Mina Haddad: Retry policy: Hosted gateway retries 2x on connect reset for streaming routes. With tighter upstream timeout, that’s probably amplifying.
We can flip retry-on to exclude `timeout` quickly (config push) but rollback may be cleaner.

Peter Holtz: Confirmed gateway pods are healthy but upstream timeouts climbed immediately post-deploy. No fleet saturation.
I vote rollback to v2.18.2. Risk: minimal.

Jordan Lee: Rollback is safe. I can initiate. Need IC approval.

Rafael Mendes: Approved. Roll back gateway v2.18.3 in us-east-1 now.

Jordan Lee: Running rollback. ETA ~10m.

Markus Klein: I’m on standby. If Hosted customer-facing, do we want a status page entry? At least "Investigating elevated error rates" for Hosted in us-east?

Aisha Bello: Per comms guidelines, we should post once we confirm user-visible impact >5m and mitigation in progress. This has been ~15m already.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. Markus draft a short status update: "Hosted API - elevated 5xx in us-east-1 affecting chat completions". Note Dedicated not impacted.

Statuspage Bot: Draft created (pending publish): "Investigating: Increased error rates for Hosted API requests in us-east-1" (component: Hosted API)

Sergio Costa: Burn-rate still high. 5xx now 2.9% last 5m, p95 latency elevated too.
Also seeing a secondary bump in streaming disconnect events, but looks like they’re a consequence (server closing early).

Peter Holtz: One more datapoint: upstream service responds fine when it gets traffic; it’s the gateway timing out before upstream finishes handshake/first byte.

Deploy Bot: Rollback started: `api-gateway` `v2.18.2` to `prod-us-east-1` (initiated by @Jordan Lee)

Mina Haddad: I can also temporarily disable retry-on-timeout for Hosted streaming to reduce amplification. But I’ll wait 3-5 min post-rollback to avoid mixing changes.

Rafael Mendes: Hold on extra changes until we see rollback effect.

Statuspage Bot: Published: "Investigating: Increased error rates for Hosted API requests in us-east-1" https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/7qf1j2c9b1

Aisha Bello: Scribing timeline:
- 16:51 burn-rate alert
- 16:53 SEV2 declared, bridge up
- 16:55 suspected gateway deploy
- 16:58 rollback approved
- 17:02 status page posted

Deploy Bot: Rollback completed: `api-gateway` `v2.18.2` to `prod-us-east-1` (duration 8m)

Sergio Costa: Graphs turning. 5xx down to 0.6% in last 2m, burn-rate alert should clear after window rolls.
Latency also trending down.

Logan Wright: Streaming disconnect metric also dropping. Good sign.

Peter Holtz: Confirmed envoy timeout counters back to baseline. Looks like v2.18.3 changed upstream timeout/flush behavior more than expected.

Mina Haddad: Routing/retry volume normalizing now. No need for config flip.

Markus Klein: Any customer comms beyond status page? Support is already pinging from a couple tickets.

Rafael Mendes: For now status page + internal note to Support is enough. If we get enterprise escalations we can provide a short RCA: "gateway timeout regression, rolled back".
Aisha can you drop a note in #support with the status link and that Dedicated not impacted?

Aisha Bello: Yep, will do.

Sergio Costa: Burn alert: 5m window likely resolves in ~3-4 min; 1h will take longer but should stop burning once steady.

Rafael Mendes: Keep SEV2 open until we’re green for 15m and confirm no other regions. Sergio quick scan eu-west?

Sergio Costa: eu-west-1 fine. us-west-2 fine. Only us-east-1 impacted.

Rafael Mendes: Great. Once stable we’ll downgrade/close.
Action items (for postmortem stub):
- Add canary for gateway timeout changes + SLO guardrail
- Update runbook: check deploy annotations first
- Add alert correlation to deploy bot message

Aisha Bello: +1. Also capture that streaming disconnects counted as server-side in this case (not client aborts), which is why both alerts fired.

Rafael Mendes: Closing SEV2 at 17:21 if metrics remain stable.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Burn-rate alert auto-resolved for Hosted / chat.completions / us-east-1 (5m window). 1h window recovering.

Rafael Mendes: Metrics stable 15m post-rollback. Closing incident. Please attach traces + dashboard snapshots to the incident doc.

Aisha Bello: Incident doc created: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/INC/INC-2026-02-11-Hosted-Gateway-Timeout-Regression
I’ll backfill timeline + owners. Postmortem scheduling TBD.

Statuspage Bot: Resolved: "Increased error rates for Hosted API requests in us-east-1" (duration: 33m) https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/7qf1j2c9b1

Change: "http2 upstream timeout defaults + streaming flush behavior" (ref: CI pipeline #129887)

Mina Haddad: Routing looks normal from policy eval metrics. No sudden shift in model mix. Seeing retry volume up though.

Jordan Lee: Any chance this is the new timeout defaults? 504 rate started right after the annotation.

Peter Holtz: On it. Checking NGINX/envoy stats (if it’s that layer). If upstream timeout got tighter we’d see 504s + client disconnect chain.

Logan Wright: Telemetry sanity: metrics ingestion is healthy, no drops. The 5xx spike is real (correlates with traces sampled at 1%).

Sergio Costa: Trace examples show `upstream_reset_before_response_started` + `upstream_request_timeout`.
Link (internal): https://trace.redwood.ai/trace/7f3a2d2b9d0c4b6a?span=gw

Aisha Bello: Any Dedicated impact? Tier filter in SLO view shows Dedicated flat.

Sergio Costa: Dedicated looks clean. This is Hosted-only (shared gateway fleet in us-east-1) but we should double-check other regions.

Rafael Mendes: Actions:
1) Peter + Jordan: assess rollback feasibility for gateway v2.18.3
2) Mina: confirm routing not amplifying retries (thundering herd) for Hosted
3) Sergio: keep eyes on burn-rate + regional spread
4) Markus: heads up for comms if we need status page

Mina Haddad: Retry policy: Hosted gateway retries 2x on connect reset for streaming routes. With tighter upstream timeout, that’s probably amplifying.
We can flip retry-on to exclude `timeout` quickly (config push) but rollback may be cleaner.

Peter Holtz: Confirmed gateway pods are healthy but upstream timeouts climbed immediately post-deploy. No fleet saturation.
I vote rollback to v2.18.2. Risk: minimal.

Jordan Lee: Rollback is safe. I can initiate. Need IC approval.

Rafael Mendes: Approved. Roll back gateway v2.18.3 in us-east-1 now.

Jordan Lee: Running rollback. ETA ~10m.

Markus Klein: I’m on standby. If Hosted customer-facing, do we want a status page entry? At least "Investigating elevated error rates" for Hosted in us-east?

Aisha Bello: Per comms guidelines, we should post once we confirm user-visible impact >5m and mitigation in progress. This has been ~15m already.

Rafael Mendes: Agree. Markus draft a short status update: "Hosted API - elevated 5xx in us-east-1 affecting chat completions". Note Dedicated not impacted.

Statuspage Bot: Draft created (pending publish): "Investigating: Increased error rates for Hosted API requests in us-east-1" (component: Hosted API)

Sergio Costa: Burn-rate still high. 5xx now 2.9% last 5m, p95 latency elevated too.
Also seeing a secondary bump in streaming disconnect events, but looks like they’re a consequence (server closing early).

Peter Holtz: One more datapoint: upstream service responds fine when it gets traffic; it’s the gateway timing out before upstream finishes handshake/first byte.

Deploy Bot: Rollback started: `api-gateway` `v2.18.2` to `prod-us-east-1` (initiated by @Jordan Lee)

Mina Haddad: I can also temporarily disable retry-on-timeout for Hosted streaming to reduce amplification. But I’ll wait 3-5 min post-rollback to avoid mixing changes.

Rafael Mendes: Hold on extra changes until we see rollback effect.

Statuspage Bot: Published: "Investigating: Increased error rates for Hosted API requests in us-east-1" https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/7qf1j2c9b1

Aisha Bello: Scribing timeline:
- 16:51 burn-rate alert
- 16:53 SEV2 declared, bridge up
- 16:55 suspected gateway deploy
- 16:58 rollback approved
- 17:02 status page posted

Deploy Bot: Rollback completed: `api-gateway` `v2.18.2` to `prod-us-east-1` (duration 8m)

Sergio Costa: Graphs turning. 5xx down to 0.6% in last 2m, burn-rate alert should clear after window rolls.
Latency also trending down.

Logan Wright: Streaming disconnect metric also dropping. Good sign.

Peter Holtz: Confirmed envoy timeout counters back to baseline. Looks like v2.18.3 changed upstream timeout/flush behavior more than expected.

Mina Haddad: Routing/retry volume normalizing now. No need for config flip.

Markus Klein: Any customer comms beyond status page? Support is already pinging from a couple tickets.

Rafael Mendes: For now status page + internal note to Support is enough. If we get enterprise escalations we can provide a short RCA: "gateway timeout regression, rolled back".
Aisha can you drop a note in #support with the status link and that Dedicated not impacted?

Aisha Bello: Yep, will do.

Sergio Costa: Burn alert: 5m window likely resolves in ~3-4 min; 1h will take longer but should stop burning once steady.

Rafael Mendes: Keep SEV2 open until we’re green for 15m and confirm no other regions. Sergio quick scan eu-west?

Sergio Costa: eu-west-1 fine. us-west-2 fine. Only us-east-1 impacted.

Rafael Mendes: Great. Once stable we’ll downgrade/close.
Action items (for postmortem stub):
- Add canary for gateway timeout changes + SLO guardrail
- Update runbook: check deploy annotations first
- Add alert correlation to deploy bot message

Aisha Bello: +1. Also capture that streaming disconnects counted as server-side in this case (not client aborts), which is why both alerts fired.

Rafael Mendes: Closing SEV2 at 17:21 if metrics remain stable.

Incident Bot: :white_check_mark: Burn-rate alert auto-resolved for Hosted / chat.completions / us-east-1 (5m window). 1h window recovering.

Rafael Mendes: Metrics stable 15m post-rollback. Closing incident. Please attach traces + dashboard snapshots to the incident doc.

Aisha Bello: Incident doc created: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/INC/INC-2026-02-11-Hosted-Gateway-Timeout-Regression
I’ll backfill timeline + owners. Postmortem scheduling TBD.

Statuspage Bot: Resolved: "Increased error rates for Hosted API requests in us-east-1" (duration: 33m) https://status.redwood.ai/incidents/7qf1j2c9b1

title: #incidents: 1770611333
preview:
Incident Bot: :rotating_light: ALERT: Hosted API tail latency SLO burn
Service: inference-gateway
Region: us-east-1
Route: /v1/chat/completions (streaming)
Metric: p95 end-to-end latency
Current: 1.82s (baseline ~380ms)
Window: 5m
Dash: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/hosted-tail/hosted-tail-latency?var-region=us-east-1
Notes: Spike started ~2 min after canary ramp for runtime-1.18 scheduler flag in hosted-canary pool.

Aisha Bello: Looking. p95 and p99 both jumped; error rate looks flat. TTFT also up but less (TTFT p95 ~620ms from ~260ms). Queue time is the main delta.

Rafael Mendes: This is the mixed-workload scheduler canary, right? We ramped hosted-canary from 5% -> 15% at :15. Feature flag `runtime.scheduler.mixed_v1` + params from the Confluence defaults.

Connor O'Brien: Confirmed. Ramp executed via releases runbook step 3. No other deploys in us-east-1 in the last 30m.

Sean Gallagher: Let’s treat as rollback candidate unless we can isolate to a knob quickly. What’s the blast radius? only hosted-canary pool or wider?

Aisha Bello: So far only `hosted-h100-us-east-1-canary` pool. Dedicated pools stable. Main hosted pool (non-canary) looks normal.

Lena Fischer: I’m here. Can someone paste the scheduler metrics dashboard for per-class queue depth + starvation counters?

Julian Moore: Dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/sched-class/scheduler-class-metrics?var-region=us-east-1&var-pool=hosted-h100-us-east-1-canary
At spike: short queue depth oscillating 0-5, long queue depth climbing to ~70 then draining. Starvation counter for LONG increments (peaks ~18/min).

Marcus Reed: That LONG starvation counter shouldn’t climb that fast unless short traffic is saturating dispatch share. What params are set? (share + aging)

Rafael Mendes: From the flag config (hosted-canary only):
`short_dispatch_share=0.90`
`long_min_dispatch_every=64` (iterations)
`aging_half_life_ms=120000`
`token_budget_short=4096`
`token_budget_long=4096`

Omar El-Sayed: 120s aging half-life seems too slow for hosted. If long prefill is stuck behind constant short decode, queue time will dominate p95 for any long-context requests (and they still count in overall p95 when mix shifts).

Aisha Bello: Correlation check: the spike aligns with a burst of long-context prompts (p95 prompt tokens went from ~400 -> ~2.1k in the same 5m). So the long queue not draining quickly enough + aging too slow => queue time spike.

Sean Gallagher: Options:
1) Rollback flag to 5% or off
2) Adjust parameters (reduce short share / increase aging) and watch
Given it’s canary-only and error rate stable, I’m ok trying a param tweak first IF we can do it safely and quickly.

Connor O'Brien: We can change the config live for hosted-canary; takes ~2-3 min to propagate. Rollback is also easy (set flag to 0%).

Lena Fischer: Suggest: bring `short_dispatch_share` down to 0.75 and reduce `aging_half_life_ms` to 10000-15000ms. Also lower `long_min_dispatch_every` so long gets guaranteed slots sooner.

Marcus Reed: +1. The current `long_min_dispatch_every=64` is too lax for high QPS. Change to 16.

Rafael Mendes: Proposed new params:
`short_dispatch_share=0.75`
`long_min_dispatch_every=16`
`aging_half_life_ms=15000`
Keep token budgets for now.

Sean Gallagher: Do it, but also pause ramp and go back to 5% traffic until stable.

Connor O'Brien: Copy. Pausing ramp and setting canary weight back to 5% now.

Incident Bot: ReleaseBot update: canary weight change initiated
Pool: hosted-h100-us-east-1-canary
Old: 15%
New: 5%
ETA: 2m

Rafael Mendes: Param update pushed for hosted-canary. Will post when it shows up in metrics.

Julian Moore: Seeing config take effect now (scheduler config label changed). Long min dispatch every=16 reflected.

Aisha Bello: Tail is recovering. p95 dropped to ~620ms and trending down. Long queue depth draining; LONG starvation counter back to ~0-1/min.

Sean Gallagher: Good. Keep it at 5% for the next 30 min. If stable, we can re-attempt ramp later today with these params.

Sofia Ivanova: Catching up. Thanks for quick triage. We need a default param change for hosted vs dedicated; the previous default was tuned on benchmark mixes, not bursty long-context shifts.

Lena Fischer: Agree. Also: our safeguard should have prevented `aging_half_life_ms=120000` on hosted. That’s basically “no aging”.

Marcus Reed: Action item: add config bounds + warn/clip for aging half-life and long_min_dispatch. We already saw a similar edge case in load tests (long starvation) — this is basically the prod version.

Julian Moore: Another action: alert on LONG starvation counter + long queue time p95. Right now only overall latency alerted; we got lucky it was isolated.

Aisha Bello: I’ll add an incident note: rollback not required; mitigated by reducing canary weight + scheduler param adjustment.

Connor O'Brien: For the record, no rollback of runtime build. Only flag weight + params in hosted-canary. I’ll update #eng-releases with “ramp paused pending stability”.

Sean Gallagher: Follow-ups (assign owners):
- [Lena/Marcus] Update recommended hosted defaults in design doc + config template.
- [Julian/Aisha] Add alerts: starvation counter + long queue time SLO guardrail.
- [Rafael/Connor] Schedule a new ramp window after 30m stable (and keep tight rollback trigger).
- [Omar] Double-check KV cache / prefill-decode interaction wasn’t contributing (just to be sure).

Omar El-Sayed: Will do. Quick scan: no cache eviction spike. Looks like pure scheduling skew + too-slow aging.

Rafael Mendes: Marking canary stable for now. Will keep watching and report if p95 moves again.

Incident Bot: Incident status update: Mitigated
Summary: p95 latency spike in us-east-1 hosted canary pool after scheduler canary ramp. Reduced canary weight and adjusted scheduler parameters (short share + aging + long min dispatch). Metrics recovered; monitoring ongoing.
Follow-ups tracked in ENG initiative.
